### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-12
[COLOR="Red"]**This project is dead since I can't maintain all the repositories any longer**[/COLOR]. However there are some good alternatives to the repositories...

---

### Post by John.Michael.Kane on 2006-09-12
Used this repo:
```
deb http://people.ubuntu.com/~rodarvus/tseliot/linux-restricted-modules/amd64/ ./
```

This Command which backedup my current confg
```
sudo nvidia-xconfig
```

Rebooted,and all is golden.

Nice work tseliot.

---

I noticed that it said:

```
II) Module fglrx: vendor="FireGL - ATI Technologies Inc."
	compiled for 7.1.0, module version = 8.28.8
```

but the xorg version is 7.0, not 7.1. Maybe that's the problem? Also, I have a 686 kernel, and I've installed the linux-686 package. I'll force the older versions of the packages for now, but I remain excited about this, and I think it's a great idea.

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-12
> **Nicksha said:**
> No luck. I've got the Radeon 9250. After the update (xorg-driver-fglrx 7.0.0-8.28.8+2.6.15.12-1, linux-restricted-modules-2.6.15-26-686 2.6.15.12-1 and linux-restricted-modules-common 2.6.15.12-1 were updated) and restart, X failed. Here's the log:
...

I noticed that it said:

```
II) Module fglrx: vendor="FireGL - ATI Technologies Inc."
	compiled for 7.1.0, module version = 8.28.8
```

but the xorg version is 7.0, not 7.1. Maybe that's the problem? Also, I have a 686 kernel, and I've installed the linux-686 package. I'll force the older versions of the packages for now, but I remain excited about this, and I think it's a great idea.

I guess I know where the problem is.

I'll contact Adam Conrad.

Thanks for reporting

I noticed that it said:

```
II) Module fglrx: vendor="FireGL - ATI Technologies Inc."
	compiled for 7.1.0, module version = 8.28.8
```

but the xorg version is 7.0, not 7.1. Maybe that's the problem? Also, I have a 686 kernel, and I've installed the linux-686 package. I'll force the older versions of the packages for now, but I remain excited about this, and I think it's a great idea.

### Post by Slychilde on 2006-09-15
Hmm, hosed my Xserver or whatever when I tried it.  I'm using a Leadtek 6600GT with 26-k7 kernel.

I put the repository in sources.list and did sudo apt-get update, sudo apt-get upgrade and the 4 new packages were found, nvidia, restricted modules, etc.  Then I did sudo nvidia-xconfig and restarted.  It restarted into commandline.  I checked /etc/X11/xorg.conf and it was using the "nvidia" module like before.

How do I undo this?

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-15
> **Slychilde said:**
> Hmm, hosed my Xserver or whatever when I tried it.  I'm using a Leadtek 6600GT with 26-k7 kernel.

I put the repository in sources.list and did sudo apt-get update, sudo apt-get upgrade and the 4 new packages were found, nvidia, restricted modules, etc.  Then I did sudo nvidia-xconfig and restarted.  It restarted into commandline.  I checked /etc/X11/xorg.conf and it was using the "nvidia" module like before.

How do I undo this?


1) did you restart your computer (not only the Xserver)?

[SIZE="3"][COLOR="Red"]NOTE: don't do this if you have a wireless card[/COLOR][/SIZE]
2) if you already did, remove those packages with:
```
sudo apt-get --purge remove nvidia-glx linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
```

then remove my repositories and install those packages again (from Ubuntu's repositories).

The module was loaded.

Did you have the same problem with the previous driver you were using?

I need to know that so as to understand if it's only the driver I packaged or a problem with your card (which might be solved with some tweaking)

### Post by Slychilde on 2006-09-15
Yessir, I was.  I'll try my best to remember exactly what I typed:

copied "deb [http://www.webalice.it/albertomilone/ubuntu/driver/32bit](http://www.webalice.it/albertomilone/ubuntu/driver/32bit) binary/" into my sources.list

```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade #noticed update-manger found the 4 packages
sudo nvidia-xconfig
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf #checked 'nvidia' module was selected
```

Gnome was still working at this point.  Then, I rebooted my pc, and got dropped to CLI.  I commented out your website,and did

```
dmesg > dmesg_output.txt
sudo apt-get --purge remove nvidia-glx linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
sudo nvidia-xconfig
```

So, when I did dmesg your repo was commented out, but the updated drivers had not been purged yet, if that helps at all.  Did I install the new 'test' drivers wrong, by chance?  I'm back in gnome with the 26-k7 kernel and ubuntu nvidia repo drivers right now, fully working (tested using warcraft III -opengl).  I'm on messenger if it would be eaiser/more helpful for you.  Cheers!

Cheers!

Can you try installing the drivers from my repos again and booting with the "nv" driver in your xorg.conf

Then (after Ubuntu has booted) Set the driver to "nvidia" in your /etc/X11/xorg.conf
Log out and Press CTRL+ALT+F1

You will get to the command line (no GUI)
Type:

```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop (if you use GDM)
```
OR
```
sudo /etc/init.d/kdm stop (if you use KDM)
```

Then:
```
startx -- -verbose 5 -logverbose 5
```

You will see several lines with the same error.

Then if you can't get to the command line press CTRL+ALT+F1 or CTRL+C (select NO if it asks you if you want to see the output of the error or something like that)

Then type this in order to copy the output of the error to your home folder:
```
sudo cp /var/log/Xorg.0.log /home/your_username/Xorg.0.log
```
(Of course you have to replace "your_username" with your username)

Access the GNOME or KDE again in the following way:
```
sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
Set the driver back to "nv" instead of "nvidia"
CTRL+O to save
CTRL+X to exit

Then
```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart (if you use GDM)
```
OR
```
sudo /etc/init.d/kdm restart (if you use KDM)
```


Post the content of that file (which you can find under /home/your_username/Xorg.0.log )Boot with the "nv" driver in your xorg.conf

Then (after Ubuntu has booted) Set the driver to "nvidia" in your /etc/X11/xorg.conf
Log out and Press CTRL+ALT+F1

You will get to the command line (no GUI)
Type:

```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop (if you use GDM)
```
OR
```
sudo /etc/init.d/kdm stop (if you use KDM)
```

Then:
```
startx -- -verbose 5 -logverbose 5
```

You will see several lines with the same error.

Then if you can't get to the command line press CTRL+ALT+F1 or CTRL+C (select NO if it asks you if you want to see the output of the error or something like that)

Then type this in order to copy the output of the error to your home folder:
```
sudo cp /var/log/Xorg.0.log /home/your_username/Xorg.0.log
```
(Of course you have to replace "your_username" with your username)

Access the GNOME or KDE again in the following way:
```
sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
Set the driver back to "nv" instead of "nvidia"
CTRL+O to save
CTRL+X to exit

Then
```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart (if you use GDM)
```
OR
```
sudo /etc/init.d/kdm restart (if you use KDM)
```


Post the content of that file (which you can find under /home/your_username/Xorg.0.log )

### Post by BLTicklemonster on 2006-09-15
The absolute sweetest thing about this is, if you ever upgrade and can't get to your desktop because /etc/X11/xorg or whatever, just enter your user name and password, then type 

envy

and run it all over again.

I ran the two aticonfig commands even but can't seem to get it to correctly determine my drivers. :/

Can you try replacing your /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2

with this file:
[http://files.covertprestige.info/important/libGL.so.1.2](http://files.covertprestige.info/important/libGL.so.1.2)

Then reboot.

If that works I can modify the driver

---

### Post by RSL on 2006-09-15
Ta-da! That did the trick.

```
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9200 Series DDR Generic
OpenGL version string: 1.3.1091 (X4.3.0-8.28.8)

```

You rock!

---

### Post by Nicksha on 2006-09-15
Just to report that everything is working now, and I didn't have to mess with libGL.

Unfortunately, the new driver seems to introduce an annoying bug for me: overlay on tv-out stretches the top part of the picture, and cuts off the bottom. But, it has nothing to do with your package of the drivers; the same happens when I install the driver with the ATI installer.

So, it's Radeon 9250, 32-bit (686 kernel) and 3d works, and it's much faster than before (1742.601 fps in glxgears). Thanks!

---

### Post by RSL on 2006-09-15
tseliot, I too am getting a lot faster and more responsive video after using your drivers. You rock and rock and rock!

---

### Post by mccarthy on 2006-09-15
Hey I tried this out and it didn't work until I replaced my libGL.so.1.2 with the one you provided.  Then it worked, but glxgears gives me around 300 fps, which is much lower than normal (I forget what I normally get).  I understand that glxgears isn't a benchmark tool but I just want to be sure that the driver is working properly.  I have an ATI Radeon X850 XT PCI-e on 32 bit ubuntu server version with fluxbox.

---

### Post by mobilehavoc on 2006-09-15
I added the repository from the first page of the thread and went through software-update and got the following - the update to the linux-restricted-modules threw me off since I thought you'd just have to update the driver.

Should I go ahead with the install for all of these, avoid updating some? Any advice appreciated.



[[IMG]http://img139.imageshack.us/img139/6829/screenshot1tu2.png[/IMG]](http://imageshack.us)

### Post by mccarthy on 2006-09-15
Ok on further investigation I was worried about nothing.  The driver works great.  By the way, thanks for this: I was having a hard time getting the latest drivers working through the ATI installer.

### Post by wuff on 2006-09-15
> **stoer said:**
> Sure, would you mind telling me how?
Thnx. Steve..

Replace like this:

cd /usr/lib
cp libGL.so.1.2 libGL.so.1.2_orig
wget [http://files.covertprestige.info/important/libGL.so.1.2](http://files.covertprestige.info/important/libGL.so.1.2)

(If it doesn't work you have the original libGL.so.1, and can copy it back with
cp libGL.so.1.2_orig libGL.so.1.2
)

Again, great job!!! =D> =D> =D>

### Post by SpectralDesign on 2006-09-15
So.. after the trouble mentioned in the post above, I ran the *.run file from ATI and told it to generate distribution specific packages, i.e.:

```

./ati-driver-installer-8.28.8.run --buildpkg Ubuntu/6.10

```

....and installed the xorg-driver-fglrx.deb package it generated....

The I rebooted -- after that I couldn't even get GDM going! (looking through the errors it looks like the distro-specific package built the fglrx driver for xorg-7.1.0 causing trouble.)

So, from the tty I just let the ati-driver-installer-8.28.8.run do what it wanted to do...

Then I:
```

sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart

```
and now I get:
```

!fgl
fglrxinfo
Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":1.0".
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9700 PRO Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6011 (8.28.8)

```

So I don't know why the ubuntu packages are messing up my system so much, but just letting the driver from ATI do it's thing seems to work best for me!

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-16
> **Ausus said:**
> Hi tseliot,

I'm doing something wrong.
I installed Kubuntu 386 version got all updates for packages.
Ugrade linux kernel fro 386 to K7.
My cpu is a dual-core AMD64. I want to use the dual-core cpu.

Code:
[HTML]uname -r[/HTML]
2.6.15-26-k7

I followed your guide.

I added repositories to source.list for  ......./32bit binary/.

Code:
[HTML]sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx[/HTML]

Done.

Code:
[HTML]sudo nvidia-xconfig[/HTML]

Done.

When restarting, I cant get to desktop.

Edit xorg.conf file change 
[HTML]Driver		"nvidia"[/HTML]
to
[HTML]Driver		"nv"[/HTML]
after I make his change I can get back to desktop.

regards](*,)
Type:
```
sudo apt-get install linux-k7
```

and it will work (that will install the restricted modules for your k7 kernel)

> When should I try this? at what point? Will this get the 8.28 drivers or what? Your answer is too vague

I'm not an expert of the ATI drivers (I can only package them or tweak them a bit) but you can read this guide:
[http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide)

EDIT: you already have version 8.28.
The opengl version is a different version (for compatibility reasons).

If you want opengl version 8.28 you can type:
make sure that you have got /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2
then:
```
sudo cp /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2.orig /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2
```

```

Be right back -- rebooting....
It was linked to a folder which doesn't exist only because of a typo:
```
/usr/lib/xorg/modules/dri -> /usr/li[COLOR="Red"]n[/COLOR]/dri
```
as it's not "lin" but "lib"

### Post by SpectralDesign on 2006-09-16
> 
People who are now using 8.28.8...do you have the issue where if you try to Log Out it dumps you to a black screen? I had the problem with 8.25.18 but I thought the newer ATI drivers were supposed to fix it..doesn't look like it. Annoying as hell 

There is a workaround mentioned in the bugzilla tracker... here are some more details:

[http://ati.cchtml.com/show_bug.cgi?id=239]("http://ati.cchtml.com/show_bug.cgi?id=239")

In short:

**GDM workaround:**
An effective workaround on my machine has been to set AlwaysRestartServer=true
in /etc/gdm/gdm.conf. Using this, GDM has not frozen at all any more, and I can
still access my text consoles regularly. The documentation for this option in
gdm.conf is "If you are having trouble with using a single server for a long
time and want gdm to kill/restart the server, turn this on."

**KDE workaround:**
For the KDM-Configuration (/etc/kde3/kdm/kdmrc in Debian)
exists an equivalent Config-Directive named 'TerminateServer'
for the Section '[X-:*-Core]':
(found in the deep of the Config Docu's etc.)

Before:
...
[X-:*-Core]
AllowNullPasswd=true
AllowShutdown=Root
NoPassEnable=false
NoPassUsers=
ServerArgsLocal=-dpi 72 -br -nolisten tcp
ServerCmd=/usr/X11R6/bin/X
...

After:
...
[X-:*-Core]
AllowNullPasswd=true
AllowShutdown=Root
NoPassEnable=false
NoPassUsers=
ServerArgsLocal=-dpi 72 -br -nolisten tcp
ServerCmd=/usr/X11R6/bin/X
TerminateServer=true
...


I checked with 'ps ax|grep /usr/X11R6/bin/X' that the X-Server was fully
exited and a new restarted (new PID).

fglrx-Driver 8.20.8 on my Setup with the 'TerminateServer' - Directive survied
many X-Session restarts without Lockups.... eventually Problem solved... 22

*Hopefully this will help you out -- I've heard from at least one person that this DID NOT work for him, but for me -- I haven't had the system hang-up since I applied the GDM fix!*

---

### Post by pcolamar on 2006-09-17
To whom it may concern:
 
I tried the method of tseliot after I had a problem with xgl/compiz upgrade that apparently screwed my ATI driver installed manually via [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide#Method_2:_Generating.2FInstalling_Ubuntu_packages_for_the_8.28.8_drivers_in_Ubuntu_Dapper_Manually]("http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide#Method_2:_Generating.2FInstalling_Ubuntu_packages_for_the_8.28.8_drivers_in_Ubuntu_Dapper_Manually")

As said, I tried tselliot (apparently) without success till I remembered that  I had blacklisted "fglrx" in accordance with ATI website in order to use the propretary driver.

Well, having "de-blacklisted" the fglrx solved the issue.

> palmer@palmer-laptop:~$ fglrxinfo
Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":1.0".
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: ATI Mobility Radeon X1600 Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6011 (8.28.8)


I am only wondering what the DRI issue is on display :1.0 

Hoping it may help somebody.
Cheers

### Post by BC12398 on 2006-09-18
I had the same issue.  KDM froze at black screen 80% of the time at shutdown.  I applied the below mentioned fix and now it has not frozen once in over 10 restarts.  Thanks much for finding this and posting it  :)  Oh and thank you TS for making this it so easy to get the ATI drivers, so far seem to be working flawlessly  :)

> **SpectralDesign said:**
> There is a workaround mentioned in the bugzilla tracker... here are some more details:

[http://ati.cchtml.com/show_bug.cgi?id=239]("http://ati.cchtml.com/show_bug.cgi?id=239")

In short:

**GDM workaround:**
An effective workaround on my machine has been to set AlwaysRestartServer=true
in /etc/gdm/gdm.conf. Using this, GDM has not frozen at all any more, and I can
still access my text consoles regularly. The documentation for this option in
gdm.conf is "If you are having trouble with using a single server for a long
time and want gdm to kill/restart the server, turn this on."

**KDE workaround:**
For the KDM-Configuration (/etc/kde3/kdm/kdmrc in Debian)
exists an equivalent Config-Directive named 'TerminateServer'
for the Section '[X-:*-Core]':
(found in the deep of the Config Docu's etc.)

Before:
...
[X-:*-Core]
AllowNullPasswd=true
AllowShutdown=Root
NoPassEnable=false
NoPassUsers=
ServerArgsLocal=-dpi 72 -br -nolisten tcp
ServerCmd=/usr/X11R6/bin/X
...

After:
...
[X-:*-Core]
AllowNullPasswd=true
AllowShutdown=Root
NoPassEnable=false
NoPassUsers=
ServerArgsLocal=-dpi 72 -br -nolisten tcp
ServerCmd=/usr/X11R6/bin/X
TerminateServer=true
...


I checked with 'ps ax|grep /usr/X11R6/bin/X' that the X-Server was fully
exited and a new restarted (new PID).

fglrx-Driver 8.20.8 on my Setup with the 'TerminateServer' - Directive survied
many X-Session restarts without Lockups.... eventually Problem solved... 22

*Hopefully this will help you out -- I've heard from at least one person that this DID NOT work for him, but for me -- I haven't had the system hang-up since I applied the GDM fix!*

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-18
> **titaniumlou said:**
> Hi, I've gone through the steps listed but cannot get my fglrxinfo to list the ATI drivers instead of the Mesa ones...

Try with:
```
sudo nano -w /etc/default/linux-restricted-modules-common
```

and make sure that

DISABLED_MODULES=""

doesn't have the word "fglrx" between the ""

---

However, after a restart X complains about a version mismatch between the kernel and the driver module. Have I done something silly like install the wrong version of the kernel headers?

Also, I've just realised I'm talking about nvidia's own driver and not the ubuntu one...

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-18
> **scumble said:**
> I've installed the 8774 nvidia drivers seemingly successfully - dropped out of X and ran the nvidia installer which compiled the kernel module with no problems.
Why did you do that?

I bet you got a driver mismatch.

Remove the restricted-modules

```
sudo aptitude purge linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
```

then run the nvidia installer but this time use the "--uninstall" function:
```
sudo sh name_of_the_installer --uninstall
```

then reinstall the driver from the repo by following the instructions on this thread.

---

### Post by toasted on 2006-09-18
Ya know, that looks a lot like the errors I got in edgy after 40 some updates broke my video. In the Edgy ATI install this is part or it:

> In Ubuntu Edgy the Composite extension is enabled by default, however, fglrx does not yet support Composite with DRI. In order to to disable Composite you have to edit the xorg.conf file:

sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf

and add these lines at the end of the file:
File: /etc/X11/xorg.conf

```
Section "Extensions"
        Option  "Composite" "0"
EndSection
```

Now, when mine broke, these 3 lines had disappeared from my xorg.conf and adding them back in renewed my system. Maybe you should try them in yours if composite is enabled.

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-19
> **gabrielsaldana said:**
> Yes, I too upgraded the kernel and synaptic doesnt have an option to reinstall the drivers.

How do you reinstall the drivers installed from the repositories?

Also...i've been having connection problems with the repository, has it changed or is it down?

to reinstall the driver type:
```
sudo apt-get install --reinstall linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r` 
```

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-19
[COLOR="Red"][SIZE="4"]UPDATE[/SIZE][/COLOR]

The kernel upgrade breaks my restricted modules therefore I suggest you either not to upgrade for a while or to set the driver to "vesa" or "nv" or "ati" in the Section "Device" of your xorg.conf (you can access the file by typing "sudo nano -w /etc/X11/xorg.conf". Press CTRL+O to save the file and CTRL+X to exit).

I will recompile the packages later today but I need to go to the University now.


I apologise for the inconvenience.


Alberto

---

### Post by Jolly Roger on 2006-09-19
I too updated the kernel before realizing what affect it might have. After I restarted my computer everything was super slow. I figured the driver got messed up so I was now running XGL on the old MESA driver. So I logged out of my separatate XGL session and went back to Gnome. Everything at least worked there. 

So fglrxinfo was giving me the MESA stuff again. I was feeling adventurous so I tried to fix the issue myself. Big mistake. I uninstalled the driver. Reinstalled it and followed all of the steps I took to originally get it to work. I then tried them all again to make sure I did everything the same way I did it before. I still had the MESA issue.

I then saw that you, tselliot, posted about reinstalling the restricted modules to fix the kernel update issue. I did that but I still have the MESA issue. 

Should I now reinstall the drivers again since I've updated the kernel module? Is my separate XGL session somehow stopping the driver from working now? I really hope I don't have to uninstall XGL and install it again. Please guide me to the solution.

EDIT: Okay, so after reading your update about the kernel breaking the restricted modules I feel a little better. I guess I didn't do anything wrong. Thanks for working on the drivers to fix all of my issues.

### Post by BLTicklemonster on 2006-09-19
I upgraded and had problems, so all I did was reboot into recovery and at the prompt typed 

envy

and installed the drivers all over again. It worked like a charm with only a few error messages, but nothing is amiss.

This also works when going between different kernels.

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-19
[SIZE="3"][COLOR="Red"]UPDATE the new repos are the following:[/COLOR][/SIZE]

For Ubuntu 32bit
```
deb http://www.albertomilone.eu/ubuntu/nvidia/packages/32bit binary/
```

For Ubuntu 64bit
```
deb http://www.albertomilone.eu/ubuntu/nvidia/packages/64bit binary/
```

Consiglia: nvidia-glx | nvidia-glx-legacy, avm-fritz-firmware-2.6.15-27
Fornisce: nvidia-kernel-1.0.7184, nvidia-kernel-1.0.8774
Descrizione: Non-free Linux 2.6.15 modules on AMD K8
 This package provides restricted modules for Linux version 2.6.15 on AMD K8. Currently the following modules are
 included:
 * madwifi (Atheros)
 * fglrx (ATI)
 * nvidia
 * fcdsl2, fcdslsl, fcdslslusb, fcdslusb, fcdslusb2, fcpci, fcpcmcia, fcpcmcia_cs (AVM ISDN)

 These modules are "restricted" because they are not available under a completely Free licence.



```

I've tryed also this (found on an ubuntu blog)
```

Try this only after verifying that you do in fact have the identified files in the stated locations.
sudo unlink /usr/lib/libGL.so.1
sudo unlink /usr/lib32/libGL.so.1
sudo rm /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2
sudo synaptic

From there just browse to fglrx and choose the "reinstall" option. Test with glxgears.

```

but no good for me.... ](*,)

```

mschievano@mschievano-desktop:~$ locate libGL.so.1
/usr/lib/libGL.so.1
/usr/lib/fglrx/libGL.so.1.xlibmesa
/usr/lib/fglrx/libGL.so.1.2.xlibmesa
/usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2
/usr/lib32/libGL.so.1
/usr/lib32/fglrx/libGL.so.1.xlibmesa
/usr/lib32/fglrx/libGL.so.1.2.xlibmesa
/usr/lib32/libGL.so.1.2
/home/mschievano/libGL.so.1.2

### Post by mobilehavoc on 2006-09-19

Thanks again for all your help. I hope future kernel updates don't always break stuff like this.

### Post by mobilehavoc on 2006-09-20

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-21
UPDATE: I have changed the repositories for bandwidth problems

*** LEGACY REPOSITORIES**:
Add this repository if you use Ubuntu Dapper 32bit:

```
deb http://www.webalice.it/albertomilone/ubuntu/driver/32bit binary/
```

OR this repository if you use Ubuntu 64bit:

```
deb http://www.webalice.it/albertomilone/ubuntu/driver/64bit binary/
```

*** NON-LEGACY REPOSITORIES**:

Add this repository if you use Ubuntu Dapper 32bit:

```
deb http://www.webalice.it/albertomilone/ubuntu/nonlegacy/32bit binary/
```

OR this repository if you use Ubuntu 64bit:

```
deb http://www.webalice.it/albertomilone/ubuntu/nonlegacy/64bit binary/
```

---

    EndSubSection

Section "ServerLayout"

        Screen          "Default Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection

```
Any suggestions?

Thank you

1) Put a # before  Option "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"  in your xorg.conf

2) type:
```
sudo nano -w /etc/linux-restricted-modules-common
```

and make sure that DISABLED_MODULES="" does not contain the word "fglrx"

#

# values from the debconf database.

# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	HorizSync    28.0 - 51.0
	VertRefresh  43.0 - 60.0
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 NP [Mobility Radeon 9600/9700 M10/M11]"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection
```

```
lspci -n | grep 300
0000:01:00.0 0300: 1002:4e50
```

Let me know if you need any more info, again cheers for the hard work.

You should try switching back to legacy repos.

1) Put the legacy repos and remove the non-legacy repos from your /etc/apt/sources.list

2) Type:
```
sudo aptitude update
```
```
sudo aptitude purge xorg-driver-fglrx
```

3) Install the previous version of the driver:
```
sudo aptitude install xorg-driver-fglrx
```

Aptitude will ask you to downgrade the restricted modules and you will need to say "yes".

Then restart the xserver after it finishes.

### Post by stoer on 2006-09-22
[I]"You should try switching back to legacy repos.

1) Put the legacy repos and remove the non-legacy repos from your /etc/apt/sources.list

2) Type:
Code:

sudo aptitude update

Code:

sudo aptitude purge xorg-driver-fglrx

3) Install the previous version of the driver:
Code:

sudo aptitude install xorg-driver-fglrx

Aptitude will ask you to downgrade the restricted modules and you will need to say "yes".

Then restart the xserver after it finishes."[/I]

tried this, i end up with the same problem but with the legacy repos's.
ie back to the whole mesa thing again.

```
 fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.4.1)

```

So now i've no idea if i had a problem with the non legacy drivers or not, seeing as with the legacy driver i get the same output.

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-22
> **barney_1 said:**
> Thanks for your hard work getting these repos set up and answering all the questions that go along with them.

I have installed the newest build (8.29.6) and still have the same problem:  My system hard freezes at a black screen just before the loging screen.  Any tips on getting around this?

ATI Radeo 9800 Pro All-In-Wonder

Boot in recovery mode and try editing your /etc/X11/xorg.conf (by typing sudo nano -w /etc/X11/xorg.conf) and remove this line 

```
Load "extmod"
```

and optionally replace it with this 

```
SubSection "extmod" 
     Option "omit XVideo" 
     Option "omit XVideo-MotionCompensation" 
     Option "omit XFree86-VidModeExtension" 
EndSubSection
```

---

### Post by veloct on 2006-09-24
ATI 9550:

I added the repo and updated as stated in the original post.  It installed with no issues and when I rebooted it was running MESA until I changed the libGL.so.1.2 library that was posted.  Now it's fine.

```

tao@sauron:~$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9550 Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6065 (8.29.6)


```

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-24
> **veloct said:**
> ATI 9550:

I added the repo and updated as stated in the original post.  It installed with no issues and when I rebooted it was running MESA until I changed the libGL.so.1.2 library that was posted.  Now it's fine.

```

tao@sauron:~$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9550 Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6065 (8.29.6)


```

Ok, it looks like I'll have to recompile the packages and include that library

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-24
> **drayan69 said:**
> eg@ubuntu:~$ lspci -n | grep 300
0000:01:00.0 0300: 1002:524c

It doesn't seem to be supported as it's not included in this list:
[http://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/1.0-8774/README/appendix-a.html](http://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/1.0-8774/README/appendix-a.html)

Others in this thread had similar systems and have gotten going. I have tried the same fixes to no avail. What is the correct source file at this time?

### Post by TrinitronX on 2006-09-25
I've got an ATI Radeon 9800pro 128MB.  Ever since the last kernel update, my fglrx drivers have been broken.  I had the last version ( 8.28.8 ) installed and working, after updating to kernel image 2.6.15-27-686, fglrxinfo reported mesa.  I attempted re-installing the 8.28.8 drivers, then tried installing the 8.29.6 ones by the old '.run --buildpkg' method with a recompiling of the driver's kernel source.  Neither worked.  I just tried installing them using the non-legacy repository, still mesa is reported.  I then replaced my libGL.so.1.2 file with the one mentioned in a previous post, and still not working.
I've rebooted after every re-install, even attempted compiling a custom kernel to install the drivers, still even that failed and reported mesa.  I'm giving up for now, I can't figure it out.

### Post by TrinitronX on 2006-09-26
Phew!  That was intense!
I first tried the libGL.so.1.2 file, but that didn't work after rebooting, so:
I first trashed my xorg.conf, ran `dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg`, edited the xorg.conf to have no errors and to make my primary screen the right one.  Rebooted, and no fglrx, but I had the right screen resolution and primary display again :P.  Then I decided to take out all unofficial repositories I had in my sources.list, update apt, reinstall many many packages, including the new kernel image, the linux-restricted-modules package, libgl1-mesa*, and a bunch of other related things.
Then I uninstalled xorg-driver-fglrx, and kernel-source-fglrx*, then rebooted.  After that, I put your repo back into my sources.list, edited the /etc/default/linux-restricted-modules-common file to un-blacklist fglrx (probably my problem).  Then I re-installed xorg-driver-fglrx and the linux-restricted-modules from your repo, rebooted, and fglrxinfo reports:
```
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9800 Pro Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6065 (8.29.6)

```

Hooray!

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-26
> **TrinitronX said:**
> After that, I put your repo back into my sources.list, edited the /etc/default/linux-restricted-modules-common file to un-blacklist fglrx (probably my problem).
That was the problem

### Post by ogami1972 on 2006-09-27
well, if a reinstall won't be too painful, it will give you a nice clean slate to start from. here's what i do when i crash X.

1. ctrl+alt+f1 (maybe unecessary)
2. sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
3. select the 'ati' driver (for now)
4. finish. reboot.

yes ,sometimes, it doesn't work. IMHO, though, with time and patience, you will figure out what's wrong, and feel better when you fix it than you would after a reinstall!

Good luck!

Meanwhile- have followed steps on page 1. X did not crash, but i am still experiencing 'the mesa issue'. will update as necessary](*,)

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-28
> **Kanybus said:**
> Ok still failed to start X server... re-install?

Boot in RECOVERY MODE from the GRUB Menu (select it using your keyboard) almost as soon as you turn on your computer (it will take you to the command line).

Then you will need to type:
```
dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

and select the "ati" driver. Press ENTER whenever you don't know the answer to a question.

Type:
```
reboot
```

On next reboot the Xserver should work fine

---

### Post by drayan69 on 2006-09-28
Thanx 4 response but mine is ATI RAGE 128 card and the link suggested by you is for nvidia drivers.

> **tseliot said:**
> It doesn't seem to be supported as it's not included in this list:
[http://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/1.0-8774/README/appendix-a.html](http://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/1.0-8774/README/appendix-a.html)

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-28
> **drayan69 said:**
> Thanx 4 response but mine is ATI RAGE 128 card and the link suggested by you is for nvidia drivers.

ATI RAGE 128 is not supported by fglrx (the proprietary driver).

You should use the "ati" (open source) driver instead (which is automatically installed with Ubuntu)

---

Edgy Eft uses XOrg 7.1 
Of course the driver for Dapper won't be compatible with Edgy.
Can you post the link with Gandalf's howto?

It's [here]("http://gandalfn.wordpress.com/howto-compiz-aiglx-on-dapper/"). It's meant to enable AIGLX in Dapper; I know Edgy uses 7.1, but apparently to get AIGLX stuff on Dapper you need to upgrade to the latest xorg-air from gandalfn's repos (NOT from official Dapper repos), whose minor is (unfortunately!) 1.

---

> **Kanybus said:**

### Post by tseliot on 2006-09-29
> **davidcie said:**
> It's [here]("http://gandalfn.wordpress.com/howto-compiz-aiglx-on-dapper/"). It's meant to enable AIGLX in Dapper; I know Edgy uses 7.1, but apparently to get AIGLX stuff on Dapper you need to upgrade to the latest xorg-air from gandalfn's repos (NOT from official Dapper repos), whose minor is (unfortunately!) 1.

I think you should ask him since I've never tried that (and I guess it could break your Dapper installation). 

AIGLX is included in XOrg.7.1 therefore I suggest you to try Edgy and use one of the tutorials which you can find in the Edgy section of this forum. I have tried AIGLX on Edgy on my old laptop (and Xfce) and it works great

### Post by Malac on 2006-10-01
Can I just say thanks tseliot.
This solved a problem after I did an upgrade to the 8.29.6 ATI prop. drivers without checking the supported hardware list.
My 9200SE was running okay on the 8.28.8 drivers but I upgraded and this crashed my xserver completely.
I tried to revert back to the 8.28.8 driver but it *would not* work. I kept getting the infamous mesa issue and no direct rendering.
I added you're repos and installed from there now it is working actually better than before with about an extra 80 fps from glxgears which doesn't sound much until you take into account I have gone from 812 fps average before the ill-fated upgrade to 895 fps after installing from your repos.
Thanks a million.
Here are my glxgears results if they are any use.
```
4506 frames in 5.0 seconds = 901.154 FPS
4503 frames in 5.0 seconds = 900.496 FPS
4502 frames in 5.0 seconds = 900.346 FPS
4458 frames in 5.0 seconds = 891.461 FPS
4439 frames in 5.0 seconds = 887.746 FPS
4439 frames in 5.0 seconds = 887.616 FPS
4471 frames in 5.0 seconds = 894.112 FPS
4503 frames in 5.0 seconds = 900.528 FPS
4503 frames in 5.0 seconds = 900.599 FPS
4503 frames in 5.0 seconds = 900.542 FPS
```And fglrxinfo :
```
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9250/9200 Series DDR Generic
OpenGL version string: 1.3.1091 (X4.3.0-8.28.8)
```Anything else I can do just let me know.

### Post by statikeffeck on 2006-10-01
**WORKS.**

I have an ATI Radeon X850XT PCI-express card on a 32-bit system. I did a fresh install of Ubuntu 6.06 today and noticed that I couldn't boot up to graphics mode from the Live CD unless I selected "safe VGA mode". After installing to the HD, however, it booted up fine (albeit with unaccelerated video).

I followed this guide and it seems the video runs much quicker now (though I only tested it with the 3D screensavers).

```
statik@BLACK:~$ lspci -n | grep 0300
0000:01:00.0 0300: 1002:5d52
statik@BLACK:~$ lspci | grep VGA
0000:01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: ATI Technologies Inc R480 [Radeon X850XT (PCIE)] (Primary)

```

I have attached the console log of my install session in case anyone wants to compare.

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-01
> **Gio2k said:**
> Yes, i do.

Well, there is way.

To go back to your old driver you will have to:
```
sudo aptitude purge xorg-driver-fglrx
```

then **remove** my repos from your /etc/apt/sources.list

```
sudo aptitude update
```
```

sudo apt-get install linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`=2.6.15-27-5
```

```
sudo aptitude install xorg-driver-fglrx
```

---

### Post by John.Michael.Kane on 2006-10-01
Tseliot just to let you know that on a fresh install. the enduser will need the lines listed below to /usr/share/applications/NVIDIA-Settings.desktop

[Desktop Entry] Name=NVIDIA Settings Comment=NVIDIA X Server Settings Exec=nvidia-settings Icon= StartupNotify=true Terminal=false Type=Application Categories=Application;System;

Note: This is only needed if your going to use this guide frist. for those who are upgrading after using this [http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper](http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper) you should be fine.

Hope this helps.

---

Make sure that the word "flgrx" is not in inverted comas after the word DISABLED_MODULES= in your /etc/default/linux-restricted-modules-common

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-02
> **SD-Plissken said:**
> Tseliot just to let you know that on a fresh install. the enduser will need the lines listed below to /usr/share/applications/NVIDIA-Settings.desktop

[Desktop Entry] Name=NVIDIA Settings Comment=NVIDIA X Server Settings Exec=nvidia-settings Icon= StartupNotify=true Terminal=false Type=Application Categories=Application;System;

Note: This is only needed if your going to use this guide frist. for those who are upgrading after using this [http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper](http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper) you should be fine.

Hope this helps.

Thanks for reporting

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-04
> **needlenose said:**
> Ati 9550
Just loaded everything seems to be fine

ohn@john:~$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0 screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9550 Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6065 (8.29.6)

Spoke to soon, getting a garbled log in screen and then a black screen after signing in? If I reboot and sign right away I'm fine as long as I'm on the computer.

Try this:
```
sudo cp /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2.orig /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2
```

### Post by needlenose on 2006-10-04
Try this:
Code:

sudo cp /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2.orig /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2


Got this
cp: cannot stat `/usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2.orig': No such file or directory

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-05
> **needlenose said:**
> Try this:
Code:

sudo cp /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2.orig /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2


Got this
cp: cannot stat `/usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2.orig': No such file or directory

??

Download one of these files:

if you use Ubuntu 32bit
[http://albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/libgl3229/usr/X11R6/lib/libGL.so.1.2](http://albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/libgl3229/usr/X11R6/lib/libGL.so.1.2)

If you use Ubuntu 64bit
[http://albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/libgl6429/usr/X11R6/lib64/libGL.so.1.2](http://albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/libgl6429/usr/X11R6/lib64/libGL.so.1.2)

Get to the folder in which you downloaded the file and type:

sudo cp libGL.so.1.2.orig /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2

If I try it in terminal I get this:
cp: cannot stat `libGL.so.1.2.orig': No such file or directory
john@john:~$

If I try to open the file I get an error message saying:
Couldn't display "/home/john/Desktop/libGL.so.1.2.orig".

I understand I'm probably in over my head and I do appreciate your guidence
Post the output of this command
```
ls $HOME/Desktop/*
```

---

### Post by needlenose on 2006-10-05
john@john:~$ ls $HOME/Desktop/*
/home/john/Desktop/ati-driver-installer-8.29.6.run
/home/john/Desktop/libGL.so.1.2.orig
john@john:~$ ls $HOME/Desktop/*ls $HOME/Desktop/*

### Post by DaOane on 2006-10-06
ATI Radeon 9200 Mobility on Acer Ferrari 3000LMi
Ubuntu 6.06, kernel 2.6.15-27-386, Xorg7.0.0

Untill I installed the drivers (8.28.8) from the Legacy Repository in this thread, I could not get hardware 3D-support (as many others with Radeon 9200 graphics). The new drivers seem to have changed this:

$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: MOBILITY/RADEON 9250/9200 Series DDR Generic
OpenGL version string: 1.3.1091 (X4.3.0-8.28.8)

But apparently drivers for XFree have been installed (see last line above, X4.3.0-8.28.8). fglrxinfo in a xgl/compiz session reports also a missing XFree extension for Xlib. Here 3D-acceleration appears not to work satisfactory.
Furthermore the drivers sometimes lock up (maybe by calling one of the test programmes (glxgears, glxheads)?). Only an empty, black window appears. Afterwards fglrxinfo returns no output, has to be cancelled by control + c. Restart of gdm/XServer helps. If the test programmes work, glxgears gives values around 1800-1850 FPS. I'm happy to supply more information if needed.

Cheers,

Henning

---

### Post by BlackWoxs on 2006-10-06
I have an ATI Radeon X600(RV380) - I followed the method of using the [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit) repository but could not get a satisfactory result even after copying over the libGL.so.1.2 file. I did have a previously installed Ubuntu version 8.25 of the ATI driver active.

The 

[FONT="Courier New"]aticonfig --initial[/FONT] 

command did not appear to be correctly configuring my  xorg.conf file. It appears that if a "fglrx" Device section exists this command does not work correctly - see: [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide#xorg.conf](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide#xorg.conf)

After using the command 

[FONT="Courier New"]sudo aticonfig --force --initial[/FONT]

everything is working fine. I compared the xorg.conf file from the non --force command with the one produced after using --force and it is different.

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-06
> **needlenose said:**
> john@john:~$ ls $HOME/Desktop/*
/home/john/Desktop/ati-driver-installer-8.29.6.run
/home/john/Desktop/libGL.so.1.2.orig
john@john:~$ ls $HOME/Desktop/*ls $HOME/Desktop/*

Type:
```
cd $HOME/Desktop
```
```
sudo cp libGL.so.1.2.orig /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2
```

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-06
> **BlackWoxs said:**
> I have an ATI Radeon X600(RV380) - I followed the method of using the [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit) repository but could not get a satisfactory result even after copying over the libGL.so.1.2 file. I did have a previously installed Ubuntu version 8.25 of the ATI driver active.

The 

[FONT="Courier New"]aticonfig --initial[/FONT] 

command did not appear to be correctly configuring my  xorg.conf file. It appears that if a "fglrx" Device section exists this command does not work correctly - see: [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide#xorg.conf](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Dapper_Installation_Guide#xorg.conf)

After using the command 

[FONT="Courier New"]sudo aticonfig --force --initial[/FONT]

everything is working fine. I compared the xorg.conf file from the non --force command with the one produced after using --force and it is different.

Thanks for reporting. I'll change the instructions

---

### Post by DaOane on 2006-10-06
> **tseliot said:**
> xgl and compiz are not stable. How does the driver perform in a non-XGL/Compiz system?

No problems except the crash of the driver I mentioned above (which I cannot reproduce). I will report back more experience about 3D-visualization when I had time to test it.

Update 2006-10-13:
I had bouth NViz and Mayvi running for 3D-visualization. Very good performance. The driver seems to run stable. :)

Update 2006-10-26
Driver does not work after upgrade to Ubuntu 6.10

Made change in /etc/X11/xorg.conf according to [http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide) and the native acceleration worked again.

---

### Post by xueg0i on 2006-10-06
I installed the new fglrx(8.29.6) driver from the repositories above and tried them with my Radeon 9550. It works fine for a single monitor setup, allthough the 3D acceleration seems a bit slower in comparisson with the older drivers.

However for a dual monitor setup I can't get it to work except when I run X as root. I cannot find anything particular strange in my Xorg.0.log after I tried to run it as another user. xorg.conf
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen        "screen0"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"

  # path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "GLcore"
	Load  "v4l"
	Load  "dri"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "monitor0"
	VendorName   "Compaq"
	ModelName    "Compaq V75 Color Monitor"
	HorizSync    31.5 - 69.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 100.0
	Gamma        1
	ModeLine     "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "640x480@72" 31.5 640 664 704 832 480 489 491 520 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "640x480@75" 31.5 640 656 720 840 480 481 484 500 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "640x480@85" 36.0 640 696 752 832 480 481 484 509 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@72" 50.0 800 856 976 1040 600 637 643 666 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@75" 49.5 800 816 896 1056 600 601 604 625 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@85" 56.3 800 832 896 1048 600 601 604 631 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "832x624@75" 57.3 832 864 928 1152 624 625 628 667 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "1024x768@85" 94.5 1024 1072 1168 1376 768 769 772 808 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "monitor1"
	VendorName   "Compaq"
	ModelName    "Compaq V75 Color Monitor"
	HorizSync    31.5 - 69.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 100.0
	Gamma        1
	ModeLine     "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync

	ModeLine     "1024x768@85" 94.5 1024 1072 1168 1376 768 769 772 808 +hsync +vsync

Section "Device"

  #Option "AGPMode" "4"
  #Option "AGPFastWrite" "true"
  #Option "EnablePageFlip" "True"
  screen 0
	Identifier  "head0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BoardName   "ati"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "No_Accel" "False"
	Option	    "No_dri" "False"
	Option	    "mtrr" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "ForceMonitors" "AUTO, AUTO"
	Option	    "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
	Option	    "BlockSignalsOnLock" "on"
	Option	    "ForceGenericCPU" "no"
	Option	    "UseFastTLS" "1"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "head1"
	Driver      "radeon"
	BoardName   "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "screen0"
	Device     "head0"
	Monitor    "monitor0"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "screen1"
	Device     "head1"
	Monitor    "monitor1"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600"

	EndSubSection

		Depth     24

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

```

I hope this helps a little ;)

xorg.conf
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen        "screen0"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"

  # path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "GLcore"
	Load  "v4l"
	Load  "dri"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "monitor0"
	VendorName   "Compaq"
	ModelName    "Compaq V75 Color Monitor"
	HorizSync    31.5 - 69.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 100.0
	Gamma        1
	ModeLine     "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "640x480@72" 31.5 640 664 704 832 480 489 491 520 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "640x480@75" 31.5 640 656 720 840 480 481 484 500 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "640x480@85" 36.0 640 696 752 832 480 481 484 509 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync

	ModeLine     "800x600@75" 49.5 800 816 896 1056 600 601 604 625 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@85" 56.3 800 832 896 1048 600 601 604 631 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "832x624@75" 57.3 832 864 928 1152 624 625 628 667 -hsync -vsync
	ModeLine     "1024x768@85" 94.5 1024 1072 1168 1376 768 769 772 808 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "monitor1"
	VendorName   "Compaq"
	ModelName    "Compaq V75 Color Monitor"
	HorizSync    31.5 - 69.0

Section "Device"

  #Option "AGPMode" "4"
  #Option "AGPFastWrite" "true"
  #Option "EnablePageFlip" "True"
  screen 0
	Identifier  "head0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BoardName   "ati"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
	Option	    "No_Accel" "False"
	Option	    "No_dri" "False"
	Option	    "mtrr" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "horizontal"
	Option	    "ForceMonitors" "AUTO, AUTO"
	Option	    "OverlayOnCRTC2" "1"
	Option	    "BlockSignalsOnLock" "on"
	Option	    "ForceGenericCPU" "no"
	Option	    "UseFastTLS" "1"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "head1"
	Driver      "radeon"
	BoardName   "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "screen0"
	Device     "head0"
	Monitor    "monitor0"

X runs okay, but I dont know what is wrong here. Need some help please. I have an ATI 9200 SE.

1) Which version of Ubuntu did you install (e.g. Dapper, Edgy, etc.)?
2) Which architecture do you use (32bit, 64bit, powerpc, etc.)
3) Which repository did you use?

### Post by kiransubash on 2006-10-08
Am running Dapper on 32 bit.
Used deb [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/legacy/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/legacy/32bit) binary/ according to the instructions on the first post.

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-08
> **kiransubash said:**
> Am running Dapper on 32 bit.
Used deb [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/legacy/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/legacy/32bit) binary/ according to the instructions on the first post.

Follow these steps:
```

cd $HOME
```
```
wget http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/libgl3229/usr/X11R6/lib/libGL.so.1.2
``` 
```

sudo cp libGL.so.1.2 /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2
```

Reboot

and see if it works

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-12
> **jinxen said:**
> But i had edgy yesterday to, and they worked perfectly then. But today i updated some packages  and it broke :P

Maybe you were still using a Dapper's kernel on Edgy. I wouldn't know.

the linux-restricted-modules which I package work only with 2.6.15.x kernels (i.e. Dapper's).

To install the driver on Edgy:

Remove my repositories from your /etc/apt/sources.list

then type:
```
sudo aptitude purge nvidia-glx linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
```
```
sudo aptitude install linux-generic nvidia-glx
```
```
sudo nvidia-xconfig --no-composite
```

then log out, press CTRL+ALT+Backspace and log in.

### Post by xurizaemon on 2006-10-13
Hi, here's my report. I have an Athlon XP & Radeon 9550 (R350). The card is a Legend Radeon 9550 AGP8x - AA-95500-TD11

I had installed both the ATI binary drivers and the default Ubuntu ones. The ATI binaries worked in dual-head fine, very happy with that, but the 3d was poo. It was Mesa.

The next few steps are recorded in full detail in the attachment 'ati-xorg-install.txt'.

I added your APT repos to my sources.list and updated APT.

I checked to see what packages matched "fglrx" with apt-cache search fglrx, then removed the ones which looked like the ATI binaries with
```
dpkg -r fglrx-control xorg-driver-fglrx fglrx-kernel-2.6.15-26-k7
```

Then I ran ```
apt-get -uf install xorg-driver-fglrx
``` which used your repos (I can tell because of the package signature prompt which I hadn't received on previous xorg-driver-fglrx install).

Then checked /etc/default/linux-restricted-modules, which already lacked the fglrx entry.

Rebooting and trying glxgears or fglrxinfo resulted in the same poor Mesa 3d performance. fgl_glxgears fails with 
```

Using GLX_SGIX_buffer
X Error of failed request: BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes)
  Major opcode of failed request: 144 (GLX)
  Minor opcode of failed request: 5 (X_GLXMakeCurrent)
  Serial number of failed request: 32
  Current serial number in output stream: 32

```

Results of dmesg, fglrxinfo and copy of /var/log/Xorg.0.log attached. Had to gzip some to get them up here - hope you can see 'em ;)

Thanks for the obvious effort you've made, tseliot. Any clues as to where I can go from here if I want better working (not Mesa) 3D?

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-14
> **pcolamar said:**
> 32bit k7
Try this:

```
wget http://albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/libgl3229/usr/X11R6/lib/libGL.so.1.2
```

then type tis command (without sudo):
```
cp /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2 $HOME/
```

and this one (with sudo)
```
sudo cp libGL.so.1.2 /usr/lib/
```

then log out and press CTRL+ALT+Backspace

and see if anything changes.


If you want to switch back to the previous file just type:
```
sudo cp $HOME/libGL.so.1.2 /usr/lib/
```

### Post by linuxa on 2006-10-20
First of all, thanks a lot **tseliot** for doing all of the How-Tos on graphics driver installs.

I followed your manual How-To back when I had Breezy on this Satellite 2410 Laptop with Nvidia Geforce4 420GO, and had the Graphics fully up.

Unfortunately, I have never once gotten the drivers working after the Dapper upgrade.

I've attempted today your nvidia-glx install via the apt. And I sense that I'm close to getting it working.

Unfortunately, My X server hangs when it attempts to boot into KDE. Running dmesg reveals that there's an API mismatch. The relevant parts of the output are:

[I]
[17179595.372000] nvidia: module license 'NVIDIA' taints kernel.
[17179595.388000] **** SET: Misaligned resource pointer: deded702 Type 07 Len 0
[17179595.388000] ACPI: PCI Interrupt Link [LNKG] enabled at IRQ 10
[17179595.388000] PCI: setting IRQ 10 as level-triggered
[17179595.388000] ACPI: PCI Interrupt 0000:01:00.0[A] -> Link [LNKG] -> GSI 10 (level, low) -> IRQ 10
[17179595.392000] NVRM: loading NVIDIA Linux x86 Kernel Module  1.0-8762  Mon May 15 13:06:38 PDT 2006
...
...
[17179618.360000] NVRM: API mismatch: the client has the version 1.0-8774, but
[17179618.360000] NVRM: this kernel module has the version 1.0-8762.  Please
[17179618.360000] NVRM: make sure that this kernel module and all NVIDIA driver
[17179618.360000] NVRM: components have the same version.[/I]

My Xorg.0.log shows 

[I](**) NVIDIA(0): Depth 16, (--) framebuffer bpp 16
(==) NVIDIA(0): RGB weight 565
(==) NVIDIA(0): Default visual is TrueColor
(==) NVIDIA(0): Using gamma correction (1.0, 1.0, 1.0)
(**) NVIDIA(0): Enabling RENDER acceleration
(EE) NVIDIA(0): Failed to initialize the NVIDIA kernel module! Please ensure
(EE) NVIDIA(0):     that there is a supported NVIDIA GPU in this system, and
(EE) NVIDIA(0):     that the NVIDIA device files have been created properly. 
(EE) NVIDIA(0):     Please consult the NVIDIA README for details.
(EE) NVIDIA(0):  *** Aborting ***
(II) UnloadModule: "nvidia"
(II) UnloadModule: "ramdac"
(II) UnloadModule: "fb"
(EE) Screen(s) found, but none have a usable configuration.

Fatal server error:
no screens found[/I]

Now, as far as I know, I don't have the 8762 drivers installed. Though I have manually attempted to install the nvidia drivers from their website, I did do an uninstall when I found that it wasn't working.

Just to confirm, when I run "sudo sh ./NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-8762-pkg1.run -i", it tells me that NO version of the drivers are found.

I suspect that the nvidia-glx packed with linux-restricted-modules is causing conflict with your nvidia-glx.

If you follow the "nvidia-glx" link off this page:
[http://packages.ubuntu.com/dapper/misc/linux-restricted-modules-2.6.15-25-386]("http://packages.ubuntu.com/dapper/misc/linux-restricted-modules-2.6.15-25-386")

it'll say that the nvidia-glx packed with the restricted modules version is 8762. 

I can't prevent the loading of the nv modules in linux-restricted-modules without blocking off access to your nvidia-glx too.

Can you please tell me how I could cleanly uninstall 8762, and use version 8774 of your nvidia-glx.

Thanks in advance.

---

```
sudo aptitude show linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
```

EDIT: I forgot to mention that I'm using the Legacy version of the repository, only because that the graphics card is more than 3 years old, and I didn't want to risk, Nvidia dropping support for it in the Non-legacy version.

Use the **Non-legacy** repository.

Then type:
```
sudo aptitude update
```
```
sudo aptitude dist-upgrade
```

and follow point 7 of the Problems Section of this guide of mine:
[http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper#PROBLEMS_SECTION](http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper#PROBLEMS_SECTION)

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-27
> **mdecandia said:**
> Hi,
i've updated my ubuntu to Edgy, added the testing repository for ati driver and update them.
Fglrxinfo return this output :

Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: [www.mesa3d.org](www.mesa3d.org)
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.5.1)

I had this problem since i've update ubuntu to edgy. 
May someboy help me?
Thanks

Type:
```
sudo nano -w /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

then add this to the end of the file:
```
Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

Then press CTRL+X to exit (save the file)

Then log out and press CTRL+ALT+Backspace.

Please let me know if anything changes

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-27
> **Carlos Araujo said:**
> some solution for ATI drivers?


Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: [www.mesa3d.org](www.mesa3d.org)
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.5.1)

Type:
```
sudo nano -w /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

then add this to the end of the file:
```
Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

Then press CTRL+X to exit (save the file)

Then log out and press CTRL+ALT+Backspace.

### Post by nimrod_abing on 2006-10-27
I just finished upgrading from Dapper to Edgy and immediately I noticed that my ATI Radeon 9250 3D acceleration is broken (again!). In Dapper I managed to fix this issue by replacing libGL.so.1.2 with one coming from an older ATI driver package (I can't recall which version). I still had the old libGL.so.1.2 around so I copied it into /usr/lib/, updated ld.so.cache, rebooted. Still 3D accelaration is broken.

Here are relevant info from my initial investigation:

```
nimrod@iwojima:$ grep WW /var/log/Xorg.0.log
        (WW) warning, (EE) error, (NI) not implemented, (??) unknown.
(WW) The directory "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic" does not exist.
(WW) fglrx: No matching Device section for instance (BusID PCI:1:0:1) found
(WW) fglrx(0): board is an unknown third party board, chipset is supported
(WW) fglrx(0): Probed monitor is 320x240 mm, using Displaysize 336x269 mm
(WW) fglrx(0): ***********************************************
(WW) fglrx(0): * DRI initialization failed!                  *
(WW) fglrx(0): * (maybe driver kernel module missing or bad) *
(WW) fglrx(0): * 2D acceleraton available (MMIO)             *
(WW) fglrx(0): * no 3D acceleration available                *
(WW) fglrx(0): ********************************************* *

```From this, I gather that the kernel DRM module is not being loaded automatically. So I tried to load the module manually but:

```
nimrod@iwojima:$ sudo modprobe drm
Password:
FATAL: Error inserting drm (/lib/modules/2.6.17-10-generic/kernel/drivers/char/drm/drm.ko): Cannot allocate memory

```I'm was in "Whiskey-Tango-Foxtrot" mode right after this because I have 1GB of memory and this is an add-on card and not built-in with the motherboard. So as to why it's complaining that it cannot allocate memory is beyond me:

```
nimrod@iwojima:$ free
             total       used       free     shared    buffers     cached
Mem:       1035680     643720     391960          0      39836     358984
-/+ buffers/cache:     244900     790780
Swap:      1048568          0    1048568

```It looks like it's not ATI's fault this time because it's the DRM module that's not loading. Everything used to work fine under Dapper.

Some more info:

```
nimrod@iwojima:$ grep -i drm /var/log/Xorg.0.log 
(II) Loading sub module "drm"
(II) LoadModule: "drm"
(II) Loading /usr/lib/xorg/modules/linux/libdrm.so
(II) Module drm: vendor="X.Org Foundation"
(II) Loading sub module "drm"
(II) LoadModule: "drm"
(II) Reloading /usr/lib/xorg/modules/linux/libdrm.so
drmOpenDevice: node name is /dev/dri/card0
drmOpenDevice: open result is 7, (OK)
drmOpenDevice: node name is /dev/dri/card0
drmOpenDevice: open result is 7, (OK)
drmOpenDevice: node name is /dev/dri/card0
drmOpenDevice: open result is 7, (OK)
drmGetBusid returned ''
(II) Loading sub module "fglrxdrm"
(II) LoadModule: "fglrxdrm"
(II) Loading /usr/lib/xorg/modules/linux/libfglrxdrm.so
(II) Module fglrxdrm: vendor="FireGL - ATI Technologies Inc."
(II) Loading sub module "fglrxdrm"
(II) LoadModule: "fglrxdrm"
(II) Reloading /usr/lib/xorg/modules/linux/libfglrxdrm.so

```The X module loads without problems. So no X.org configuration problems here. But just in case, here's the relevant section from my config file:

```
Section "Device"
        Identifier  "ATI Radeon 9250"
        Driver      "fglrx"
#       BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
        Option      "FSAAScale" "0"
        Option      "FSAAEnable" "off"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
#       Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        Option      "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
        Option      "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
EndSection

``````
nimrod@iwojima:$ export LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose
[~]
nimrod@iwojima:$ fglrxinfo 
Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
libGL error: XF86DRIQueryDirectRenderingCapable failed
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.5.1)

```Here is my hardware setup:

Motherboard: ECS P4VMM2 (this is 1st revision motherboard I think)
Memory: 1GB
Graphics card: PowerColor 9250 (Radeon 9250)

Here is the link to a review for my graphics card if it helps:

[http://www.pcstats.com/articleview.cfm?articleID=1638](http://www.pcstats.com/articleview.cfm?articleID=1638)

It's one of those bargain cards. This was all I could afford at the time and it had a nice ROI (good performance for its price point) plus it uses a 
passive heatsink which makes for a very silent PC.

I play most of my 3D games on Windows anyway so it's not really an issue for me. But I'm placing this report here so that others with a similar setup and experiencing the same problem can find it. It would be nice to fix this though, but it's been a long time since I mucked around in the Linux kernel sources and so much has changed, especially in the drivers, since then.

If someone is willing to fix this, I can provide more info if needed. But for now, I must get back to playing with my shiny new Ubuntu system :)

### Post by nimrod_abing on 2006-10-27
> **Jolly Roger said:**
> Nimrod and Clever did you guys try tselliot's suggestion he posted on the last page? It worked like a charm for me and I now have XGL and Beryl running.

This is not just a case of simple misconfiguration. Everything was working fine **before** I upgraded to Edgy. I had hardware accelerated 3D back then, but only because I installed a libGL.so.1.2 from an older driver package.

Here is my previous post in this thread. It seems you missed it, it has all the relevant details:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1672839&postcount=420](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1672839&postcount=420)

See also the LaunchPad bug report I added to.

As for tseliot's suggestion to disable the Composite extension, yes, I tried it just know but I already knew it would not work but still I tried it :D

To summarize, the kernel DRM module fails to load for some unknown reason. This module is required by the ATI proprietary (and Open Source) drivers as it is a prerequisite to get DRI going.

When I try to insert the drm module manually:

```
root@iwojima:~ # modprobe drm
FATAL: Error inserting drm (/lib/modules/2.6.17-10-generic/kernel/drivers/char/drm/drm.ko): Cannot allocate memory

```Strange thing is that I tried with the Open Source ati drivers and it loads DRM perfectly but then when you try to run any program that requires hardware accelerated 3D, the system locks up.

I'm not really into all this flashy beryl/compiz stuff (it only gets in my way) and I have a console when I need to play games or I just boot into Windows. But I do need hardware accelerated 3D for some other things like Blender and this data visualization program that I have been working on. But hey, as long as Java is not broken, then I'm happy :). So although it's a low priority for me, I'm still interested in getting this resolved.

Lastly, I'm not willing to try installing from ATI packages. I already have 3 "alien" packages on my system and it's a PITA to keep track of them already. Adding ATI packages to the mix will further complicate things. I would have to manually recompile the kernel with every security update. The reason I switched to Ubuntu from Gentoo, LFS, Slackware and FreeBSD (at one point I was using all four distros, 3 of them under VMWare) is that I got tired of being bothered to tinker around with config files and recompiling stuff with every update.

---

### Post by nimrod_abing on 2006-10-28
It looks like another headscratching moment for me. :confused:

I finally managed to get DRI working and strangely enough, the "Disable Composite Extension" trick worked this time. Now I got it working and fglrxinfo reports:

```
nimrod@iwojima:$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9200 Series DDR Generic
OpenGL version string: 1.3.1091 (X4.3.0-8.28.8)

```YAY! But still it bothers me that "Disable Composite Extension" did not work before. So I did a little backtracking...

It turns out, if you have been using the Open Source ATI drivers and then you tried the fglrx drivers with compositing disabled, [COLOR=Red]**it is very important that you reboot your computer. Zapping your X server won't work (CTRL+ALT+Backspace)**[/COLOR].

HTH.

---

### Post by Bo Rosén on 2006-10-28
> **tseliot said:**
> Follow this guide:

As I said, you won't get support for that driver in this thread

Thanks for the links. I know I won't get any support, I wasn't asking for any. 
I just thought someone might be interested in my progress ;-) 
Thanks for all the help and good work with this.

EDIT:  Adding this to Section "Screen" in xorg.conf solved all my troubles. Everything now works.

# Enable 32-bit ARGB GLX Visuals
        Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"

---

### Post by ayurchen on 2006-10-28
Maybe it is only me, but in the xorg-driver-fglrx version 7.1.0-8.29.6+2.6.17.6-1 from [http://albertomilone.com/drivers/edgy/nonlegacy/64bit](http://albertomilone.com/drivers/edgy/nonlegacy/64bit) binary/ (i.e. the latest 64bit driver at the moment) /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2 is a 32bit library. I verified it by upgrading and downgrading xorg-driver-fglrx package several times: every time I installed the driver from [http://albertomilone.com](http://albertomilone.com) /usr/lib/libGL.so.1.2 becomes a 32bit library (according to file output) and nothing (glxinfo, fglrxinfo, mplayer) works complaining about it.
