### Post by MrBlond83 on 2006-10-29
After having the 8.28.8 driver from the official edgy repositories, I decided to try and upgrade to the 8.29.6 using these repositories. At first, Mesa loaded, but then after I created a link to the dri library it stopped loading Mesa, and started giving me the infamous spam of "API ERROR: could not register entrypoint..." whenever I ran fglrxinfo and glxinfo.

After looking through the forums, I could not find a way to solve this problem. I found some old posts in other forums regarding old fglrx versions, where it was said to be resolved by replacing the libGL.so library with one from the previous version. Personally, this is not the solution I am looking for, since if I'm installing a newer version, which is supposed to support my card (ATI Radeon 9800 Pro), I do not want to go back and grab libraries from the older version.

If you guys think it's a known problem caused by ATI, that's fine, I will wait for the next version. But after googling around, it seems that the only users still reporting this entrypoint issue with the 8.29.6 drivers are users that installed the drivers using your repository. I would say even an average user like me can figure out it must be a problem with the libGL file in your .deb package.

If there is any other solution to this I would be happy to hear - Again, I would like to get to the bottom of this issue and find a real solution, one that does not involve copying files from older versions, since that goes against the benefits of installing a newer version in the first place. If there's no other solution, i'll happily wait for the official repositories to provide me with a newer version, since I am having no problems at all with the 8.28.8 driver (other than the lack of aiglx support, but that is not solved by 8.29.6 anyway).

---

At first, Mesa loaded, but then after I created a link to the dri library it stopped loading Mesa, and started giving me the infamous spam of "API ERROR: could not register entrypoint..." whenever I ran fglrxinfo and glxinfo.

### Post by tseliot on 2006-10-31
> **bobsta63 said:**
> I am using an Nvidia GeForge graphic card, Does this mean it affects me too?
1) What does (or would) affect you?
2) What is the exact model?

> **bobsta63 said:**
> Also my highest screen resolution that the workstaion will allow is 1024 x 768, Is this due to the GRaphics drivers or is this the highest resolution Ubuntu (6.06)supports?
If you installed the Nvidia driver you can solve the problem in the following way:
Try point 10 of the Problems Section of this guide:
[http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper#PROBLEMS_SECTION](http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper#PROBLEMS_SECTION)

Then if that doesn't solve the problem:

Try this guide:
[http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/ChangeResolution](http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/ChangeResolution)


OR this one (read where it says "Getting the Right Mode for an LCD monitor"):
[https://wiki.caosity.org/tiki-index.php?page=X%20Server%20Configuration](https://wiki.caosity.org/tiki-index.php?page=X%20Server%20Configuration)

### Post by Joe_CoT on 2006-10-31
Don't know how much of this applies to the new ati drivers in general, and how much to specifically yours (pretty sure it's in general, dunno how much you can change yours to fix these problems).

I was also getting this error
```
(EE) AIGLX error: dlsym for __driCreateNewScreen_20050727 failed (/usr/lib/dri/fglrx_dri.so: undefined symbol: __driCreateNewScreen_20050727)
(EE) AIGLX: reverting to software rendering
```

This can be disabled by adding this to your xorg.conf
```
Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection
```

This is mostly a cosmetic change, as it is essentially what happens as a result anyway. I for one welcome our ATI overlords (to release a driver that supports composite). 

The other big issue is Mesa rendering. The first cause (which you're probably all aware of by now) is composite being on by default, which breaks ATI dri. Adding this to xorg.conf:

```

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"
EndSection

```

Is part 1 of fixing that (the universal fix). However, still no dice for DRI on my amd64 pc. Running fglrxinfo like so
```
LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose fglrxinfo
```

Gave me an error saying that /usr/X11R6/lib64/modules/dri/fglrx_dri.so could not be found (don't feel like breaking it again to reproduce the error; I'm sure you'll all be able to do so ;) ). It's actually located at /usr/lib/dri/fglrx_dri.so . I fixed it with the following:
```

mkdir /usr/X11R6/lib64/
mkdir /usr/X11R6/lib64/modules
ln -s /usr/lib/dri /usr/X11R6/lib64/modules/dri

```

After that things worked just fine. The first error (aiglx) is obviously out of your hands. Could you please look into the fglrx_dri.so problem?

---

EndSection
```

This is mostly a cosmetic change, as it is essentially what happens as a result anyway. I for one welcome our ATI overlords (to release a driver that supports composite). 

The other big issue is Mesa rendering. The first cause (which you're probably all aware of by now) is composite being on by default, which breaks ATI dri. Adding this to xorg.conf:

```

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "0"

```

Is part 1 of fixing that (the universal fix). However, still no dice for DRI on my amd64 pc. Running fglrxinfo like so
```
LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose fglrxinfo
```

Gave me an error saying that /usr/X11R6/lib64/modules/dri/fglrx_dri.so could not be found (don't feel like breaking it again to reproduce the error; I'm sure you'll all be able to do so ;) ). It's actually located at /usr/lib/dri/fglrx_dri.so . I fixed it with the following:
```

mkdir /usr/X11R6/lib64/
mkdir /usr/X11R6/lib64/modules
ln -s /usr/lib/dri /usr/X11R6/lib64/modules/dri

```

After that things worked just fine. The first error (aiglx) is obviously out of your hands. Could you please look into the fglrx_dri.so problem?

### Post by kees on 2006-11-02
For anyone interested in the supported Ubuntu nvidia-glx (and linux-restricted-modules-*) for Dapper and Edgy, the pending [security update]("http://nvidia.custhelp.com/cgi-bin/nvidia.cfg/php/enduser/std_adp.php?p_faqid=1971") is currently being tested.

We'd like to get wider testing of the supported nvidia drivers package, so if you still have the stock Dapper or Edgy nvidia drivers installed and want to help test the 8776 driver release, please read:

[https://lists.ubuntu.com/archives/ubuntu-devel/2006-November/022177.html](https://lists.ubuntu.com/archives/ubuntu-devel/2006-November/022177.html)

By adding:

```
deb http://people.ubuntu.com/~kees/testing/ ./
```

to your /etc/apt/sources.list, the new packages should show up after an "apt-get update".  Remember you must **reboot** after doing the "apt-get upgrade" or else X will not restart (since the new nvidia kernel module will not yet be loaded).

So far, everything has tested okay, but we want to make sure with other nvidia folks on a wider set of hardware that there haven't been any unnoticed regressions before we officially release the supported drivers.  :D

Thanks!

---

You're well within your rights to be separately testing the Nvidia driver, but if you're going to advertise in this thread, could you please at least reference [your post](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=290735) about it? ### Post by tseliot on 2006-11-05
> **monkeypipi said:**
> Thank you for your help. But I cannot see the update from your stable Edgy 64 bit Repo. After I add the unstable source to my repo, I got the Linux-restricted-modules updates:-k But same problem.

The I tried to delete all libGL.so.1 and reinstalled all fglrx drivers once again.But still no luck. Am i the only one who has the problem?
[B]
If you don't use a wireless card to connect to the internet[/B] try this:
```
sudo aptitude purge linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
```

and answer yes (no matter what it asks you to remove)

then
```
sudo aptitude purge xorg-driver-fglrx
```

then use the stable repositories (nonlegacy) for Edgy.

They should work now.

and install the driver.
```
sudo aptitude install linux-generic xorg-driver-fglrx
```

### Post by clever forum name on 2006-11-05
Recent efforts to restore performance with my X600 graphics card have not been entirely successful. Here are some of the basic metrics I see being passed around:

$ lspci |grep VGA
01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: ATI Technologies Inc RV370 5B62 [Radeon X600 (PCIE)]

$ glxgears -printfps
1253 frames in 5.0 seconds = 250.404 FPS
1250 frames in 5.0 seconds = 249.984 FPS
1244 frames in 5.0 seconds = 248.784 FPS

$ fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON X600 Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6119 (8.30.3)

$ uname -rm
2.6.17-10-386 i686

I tried the standard Ubuntu fglrx first, the results are the same. After working with the configurations for a while and reading in these forums, it seemed that I needed 8.30+. I went through the steps to get the ATi proprietary software installed in Edgy. ([http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide#Method_2:_Generating.2FInstalling_Ubuntu_packages_for_the_new_8.30.3_drivers_in_Ubuntu_Edgy_Manually](http://wiki.cchtml.com/index.php/Ubuntu_Edgy_Installation_Guide#Method_2:_Generating.2FInstalling_Ubuntu_packages_for_the_new_8.30.3_drivers_in_Ubuntu_Edgy_Manually))

In all cases the software modules are installed, loaded and do not generally have any complaints or errors. The only difference I see at this point is in Dapper I was able to get 3200+ FPS, where in Edgy I am getting about 250 FPS. In Dapper I was not using the 8.30+ driver from ATI. I only went this route most recently.

Ideas or thoughts are greatly appreciated. Thanks for posting the information to get me this far.
_CFN

### Post by clever forum name on 2006-11-07
> **dave1507 said:**
> Hi,

I followed the guide on the website and everything seemed to install OK. I booted up fine and everything appears OK but I still have no 3d and it still says Mesa!

If I type fglrxinfo I get:

Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: [www.mesa3d.org](www.mesa3d.org)
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.5.1)

If I type glxinfo | grep rendering I get:

Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
direct rendering: No

glxgears also shows the same error. Any ideas how to fix this?

I have a Radeon X1600PRO 512mb AGP and Intel P4 2.8GHZ

Here is my xorg.conf:

You may only need to add the following in your xorg.conf:
Section "Extensions"
     Option "Composite" "0"
EndSection

You need to do this manually:
$sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf

If this is not included, you will fall back to "MESA-mode" despite having installed fglrx || other ATI drivers + sw correctly.
Also note that this only applies to the Edgy Eft distribution (6.10). Dapper Drake (6.06) does not require this step. I'm not sure which website you were referring to, but here is a link to the documentation that I found helpful.
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI#head-c966b2cb7c82944d6883f27a2896725db3b90a3a](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/BinaryDriverHowto/ATI#head-c966b2cb7c82944d6883f27a2896725db3b90a3a)
I suspect you will find success, but slow FPS. At leaset that is my situation. I have an ATI X600 (PCIE), all sw installed, but only 250FPS. I was fine in Dapper Drake with 3200+FPS. If you find anything on this topic, let me know. I'd be stoked.
_CFN

	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"

	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

### Post by networkslayer on 2006-11-10
> **b0rdslide said:**
> The driver from alberto also hasn't worked for me. How did you get the old ubuntu ones back in?

Hi...

Open the Synaptic-package-Control and log in as root - search for "fglrx" - I deinstalled all of the stuff... and deselected alberto's repositories

I updated my official ubuntu repos (to get the old driver) and installed the the driver...

Don't forget to reinstall the linux-restricted-modules for your system again (maybe 386 or x86_64 generic) - the official driver from the ubuntu repositories did not install that package - only xorg-drivers were listed...

Hope this helps :-k

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-11-10
> **peter07 said:**
> I've just edited my post, now it is in English :)

Ignorowanie means: ignore ( ignoring )

You might have some problem since you're ignoring a few repositories (not only mine).

Try replacing the content of your sources.list with this:

```
deb http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy main restricted
deb-src http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy main restricted

## Major bug fix updates produced after the final release of the
## distribution.
deb http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy-updates main restricted
deb-src http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy-updates main restricted

## Uncomment the following two lines to add software from the 'universe'
## repository.
## N.B. software from this repository is ENTIRELY UNSUPPORTED by the Ubuntu
## team, and may not be under a free licence. Please satisfy yourself as to
## your rights to use the software. Also, please note that software in
## universe WILL NOT receive any review or updates from the Ubuntu security
## team.
deb http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy universe
deb-src http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy universe

## Uncomment the following two lines to add software from the 'backports'
## repository.
## N.B. software from this repository may not have been tested as
## extensively as that contained in the main release, although it includes
## newer versions of some applications which may provide useful features.
## Also, please note that software in backports WILL NOT receive any review
## or updates from the Ubuntu security team.
# deb http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy-backports main restricted universe multiverse
# deb-src http://fr.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ edgy-backports main restricted universe multiverse

deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu edgy-security main restricted
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu edgy-security main restricted
deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu edgy-security universe
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu edgy-security universe

deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/  edgy universe main restricted multiverse

deb http://albertomilone.com/drivers/edgy/nonlegacy/64bit binary/

#deb http://compiz-mirror.lupine.me.uk/ edgy main-edgy main-edgy-amd64
```

### Post by juve on 2006-11-11
Here are my unsolved issues with fglrx 8.30:

```
Section "Modes"
###############################
# Welcome to ATI-Refresh-Hell #
############################### 
#  Problem 1 (since 8.25):
#        Have to use modelines because fglrx >= 8.25 can't go beyond 85 Hz
#        without them. DDC is probalbly broken
#
#  Problem 2 (since 8.30):
#        can't use this Section anymore. !!! (Problem 1 "solved")
#        using this Mode Section with my primary Monitor will stop Xorg from
#        loading, it shows a blank screen and i can't go back to tty1/2/3
#        i have to reboot in recovery mode afterwards and disable the Mode Section
#
#    Problem (general):
#        Can't use modelines for secondary Screen in BigDesktop mode
#        because you can't use a "Monitor" section for the second screen
#        you are rescricted to the HSync2 VRefresh2 values in the device Section
#        And they do not function as they should
#
#  partial solution:
#        Use higher HorizSync Values in the Monitor Section:
#        HorizSync 30-98  -> 85 Hz is maximum
#        HorizSync 70-98  -> 100 Hz is maximum
#        HorizSync 86-98  -> blank screen, grrrr!!!
#
#    !!! Proprietary drivers SUCK !!!

    Identifier     "120Hz-only"
    ModeLine     "1024x768" 136.5 1024 1072 1312 1408 768 770 782 808
    ModeLine     "800x600" 86.0 800 840 1040 1120 600 602 614 640
    ModeLine     "640x480" 55.9 640 672 832 896 480 482 494 520
    ModeLine     "640x400" 47.3 640 672 832 896 400 402 414 440
    ModeLine     "320x240" 15.1 320 336 416 448 240 242 254 280
    ModeLine     "320x200" 12.9 320 336 416 448 200 202 214 240
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
#    UseModes     "120Hz-only"
    DisplaySize  361    271
    HorizSync    70.0 - 98.0
    VertRefresh  100.0 - 150.0
    Option        "DPMS"
EndSection
```
I recommend that the fglrx-package should disable all Mode Sections/ ModeLines in xorg.conf (or at least warn the user before the installation proceeds), otherwise some user might update his box and will watch a blank screen when restarting X or rebooting the next time.

### Post by marquedios on 2006-11-14
I'm not sure how many people will actually have this problem but there is a known bug with the NV2x GPU's and the latest nvidia driver.  After doing some googling I ran across [this thread]("http://www.nvnews.net/vbulletin/showthread.php?t=80218").  Having run compiz in the past I was looking forward to actually using it this time (you know, the actual functional features) and was actually hoping to use beryl instead.  I attempted Fedora's built in AIGLX but apparently the same bug affected me there as well but didn't know it at the time.  I was wondering if you still had the 9726 version of the nvidia-glx and dependencies either laying around or compilable so that those of us that are affected by this bug could do a force version on that one and still use beryl.  Granted its an old GPU but it does do quite nice with 90% of the functional features of XGL.

Thanks in advance and nice site by the way (great idea pointing a reader to this thread)!!

### Post by bonzini on 2006-11-17
Just looking this over, and I'm a bit lost but on the other hand I may be able to provide a bit of useful info too!

I just moved away from the fglrx driver to the ati driver on my Toshiba PSA70C-RX100E (this is a 3.2Ghz Intel hyperthreaded box with an ATI Radeon Mobility 9100 U3 (R200 IGP) chipset.

This chipset has NEVER worked with fglrx properly.  If you examine the log with the fglrx driver installed, you will see that it comes up BUT the DRI is not supported with this chipset.

So, I finally decided to follow the wiki instructions under "Radeon" and "CompositeManager/AIGLX", which basically get you back into the ati driver (not the fglrx driver) and AIGLX.

The result of this seems to be working really well.  OK with one exception :-k whenever I run something like "glxinfo" I see the following warning in the midst of the other output:

libGL warning: 3D driver claims to not support visual 0x4b

Not sure what that's about...

Anyway, hope this is of interest.

### Post by steveneddy on 2006-11-18
After a complete reinstall of Dapper after trying to install Edgy, I discovered that one really needs to completely uninstall any nvidia stuff you may have installed before trying to install the new nvidia beta driver.

> 
sudo apt-get --purge remove nvidia-glx-legacy nvidia-xconfig nvidia-settings linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r` linux-restricted-modules-common


then
> 
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop


CD to the directory where you dl-ed the nvidia beta driver and

> 
sudo sh NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-9742-pkg1.run 


> 
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart


and all will be better than before - nvidia beta works better with beryl than the stock drivers you can get from the repositories at this time.

:)

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-11-18
> **steveneddy said:**
> After a complete reinstall of Dapper after trying to install Edgy, I discovered that one really needs to completely uninstall any nvidia stuff you may have installed before trying to install the new nvidia beta driver.

There's no need to remove the restricted modules to do that. And removing the restricted modules might remove the support for certain **wireless cards** (i.e. some users will **lose their internet connection**).

You should blacklist the driver instead.

Read Method 2 of my guide for Ubuntu Edgy:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=281823](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=281823)

or use Envy 0.7.2:
[http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html)


Anyway this thread is about my repositories.

# 
# deb cdrom:[Kubuntu 6.06.1 _Dapper Drake_ - Release i386 (20060807.1)]/ dapper main restricted


deb cdrom:[Kubuntu 6.06.1 _Dapper Drake_ - Release i386 (20060807.1)]/ dapper main restricted

deb-src http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper main restricted

## Major bug fix updates produced after the final release of the
## distribution.
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper-updates main restricted
deb-src http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper-updates main restricted

## Uncomment the following two lines to add software from the 'universe'
## repository.
## N.B. software from this repository is ENTIRELY UNSUPPORTED by the Ubuntu
## team, and may not be under a free licence. Please satisfy yourself as to
## your rights to use the software. Also, please note that software in
## universe WILL NOT receive any review or updates from the Ubuntu security
## team.
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper universe
deb-src http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper universe

## Uncomment the following two lines to add software from the 'backports'
## repository.
## N.B. software from this repository may not have been tested as
## extensively as that contained in the main release, although it includes
## newer versions of some applications which may provide useful features.
## Also, please note that software in backports WILL NOT receive any review
## or updates from the Ubuntu security team.
deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper-backports main restricted universe multiverse
deb-src http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper-backports main restricted universe multiverse


deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu dapper-security main restricted
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu dapper-security main restricted
deb http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu dapper-security universe
deb-src http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu dapper-security universe


deb http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper multiverse 
deb-src http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/ dapper multiverse

## PLF
## Please report any bug on https://launchpad.net/products/plf/+bugs
## wget http://packages.freecontrib.org/ubuntu/plf/12B83718.gpg -O- | sudo apt-key add -
#deb http://packages.freecontrib.org/ubuntu/plf/ dapper free non-free
#deb-src http://packages.freecontrib.org/ubuntu/plf/ dapper free non-free


##Alberto Milone's nVidia repository
# wget http://albertomilone.com/drivers/tseliot.asc
# gpg --import tseliot.asc
#gpg --export --armor albertomilone@alice.it | sudo apt-key add -
deb http://albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit binary/


## CANONICAL COMMERCIAL REPOSITORY (Hosted on Canonical servers, not Ubuntu
## servers. RealPlayer10, Opera and more to come.) 
deb http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu dapper-commercial main

## KDE 3.5.5 & Amarok 1.4.3 Kubuntu Packages
## wget http://people.ubuntu.com/~jriddell/kubuntu-packages-jriddell-key.gpg
## sudo apt-key add kubuntu-packages-jriddell-key.gpg
deb http://kubuntu.org/packages/kde-355 dapper main
deb http://kubuntu.org/packages/amarok-143 dapper main
```

no changes are made, and

Section "Extensions"

EndSection

to the xorg.conf.

### Post by tseliot on 2006-11-28
> **daller said:**
> Still no luck! - Segmentation fault! (That was with the stable version!)
You should downgrade to version 8776 because of your card.

> **daller said:**
> You state that the driver may be "dangerous" to use! - What exactly does that mean? Hardware destruction?
In theory there's a risk if you use a beta driver.

> **daller said:**
> EDIT: And what's with the version numbering? 0.7.2 is stable, and 0.6.1 is beta?

0.7.2 contains the Stable driver 9629

0.6.1 contains the old Beta driver 9626 (which works better with some cards).

> **Robbyx said:**

I downloaded Envy and installed it according to the instructions.

It failed at the end because it was not able to go to the directory at the Nvidia site known as:  

/free86/linux-x86/1.0-9631 

No such directory.

As others have used Envy with no problems I suspect I have omitted a vital stage before I ran it. All I did was to download Envy and install it. Should I have done other things before that stage?
   
The failure to get the driver has  meant that Envy aborted. 

I do not know what to do next. I had the Atomatrix2 nvidia driver installed but it was causing me a problem in that I could not access the Terminal screen from within the GDM, when I had Xinerama running. I would like to install the latest Nv non legacy 32bit driver, but currently I can not get into Ub's graphic interface. I can access the text login.

Robin
The problem is solved in the latest release of envy.

download it and install it in the following way (you can do it from the command line):
```
wget -c http://albertomilone.com/ubuntu/nvidia/scripts/envy_0.7.3-0ubuntu2_all.deb
```

then type:
```
sudo aptitude purge envy
```

```
sudo dpkg -i envy*.deb
```

and run envy again.

### Post by tseliot on 2006-12-11
> **quiquoqua said:**
> hi good boys, i'm having problems with an **ATI X200M rs480 (5955) pcie**. It's the graphic card of my notebook. the machine is a turion 64 ml-32 with 512M of DDR ram (I activated the sideport+uma=256m as suggested in many threads) The Producer is HP, model **DV8012EA**.

i would like to pimp my notebook with beryl or compiz on it, and as far as i know this (in my case) would be possible only via xgl.
i've seen some successful stories, but they refer to an xorg6.9 environment with old drivers(this means i should use dapper: i did it and no clue), but i'd like to use edgy amd64 a little more, for the wireless card compatibility.
...

It looks like AIGLX is the problem.

Try adding this to the end of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
```
Section "ServerFlags"
        Option  "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

```

### Post by tseliot on 2006-12-21
> **ischg said:**
> I have an ATI x1400 mobile chip and upgrading to the 8.30 driver did not work at all (something crashes and I end up with the mesa driver - I think other people ran into a very similar problem). 

Instead of trouble-shooting, I tried rolling back to the 8.28 version but failed miserably (by removing the repos, deleting the *.debs from the cache and uninstalling/reinstalling the fglrx drivers). Now, my machine still doesn't load the ATI drivers, and I curiously even lost my wireless adapter (ifconfig doesn't even show eth1 any longer).

To cut a long story short: I'll probably set up the system from scratch again, since I still haven't invested too much time in customizing it. But it would be great if the promised miniHOWTO for rolling back to the regular drivers were available. (I ran into this miserable driver issue twice this week)
1) Remove my repository
you should know how to do that
2) Refresh the repository list
```
sudo aptitude update
```
3) Remove (and purge) fglrx
```
sudo aptitude purge xorg-driver-fglrx
```

4) Install fglrx from Ubuntu's repositories:
```
sudo aptitude install xorg-driver-fglrx
```
Aptitude will suggest you a few solutions.

Keep saying "no" until aptitude asks you whether you want to downgrade the restricted modules or not (then say yes).

5) Then restart your computer

---

### Post by gray-squirrel on 2006-12-27
I followed these [instructions]("http://albertomilone.com/instructions.html"), then proceeded to [Method 1 On Line]("http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper#METHOD_1_-_ONLINE_INSTALLATION") installation.  I installed a new nVidia GeForce FX 5200 card a few hours ago.

(I was a little concerned because I have integrated graphics on the system board but fortunately, the BIOS detected a card, as did Kubuntu, so I was able to actually to have a Web browser handy {It's VIA's Unichrome}.  I thought I'd be at the command line for a long time but was prepared.)

I did have dependency issues pointed out to me, and I wished I had saved them for posting here.  I went ahead and had aptitude sort it out, which it did without incident.  Then I rebooted, and got 3D acceleration.

I had to reboot again, as I was also troubleshooting a problem with the new HP DVD writer I installed at the same time as the video card but could not get fully recognized, and lost 3D acceleration after logging in.  For some reason the changes made by [FONT="Courier New"]sudo nvidia-xconfig[/FONT] did not stick, so I went into the [FONT="Courier New"]xorg.conf[/FONT] file and made the changes as stated in step 4 for [Off-line installation]("http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper#METHOD_1_-_OFFLINE_INSTALLATION") - with the exception of [FONT="Courier New"]#Load "glcore"[/FONT] as the original line was never there in my [FONT="Courier New"]xorg.conf[/FONT] to begin with.  Then I saved the file, and restarted X and regained 3D.  Hopefully the changes will stick this time.

It is great to finally be able to use all 768M of RAM on my system.  And many thanks to tseliot for putting this all together.

---

### Post by flapane on 2006-12-29
@alberto

I finally made up the PSB not supported by BIOS issue with dual core so I could compile my own kernel without issues. I downloaded the last envy and it downloaded the 97xx driver version. There was a problem because it said the gcc didn't match with the kernel one (kernel 4.0 and current gcc 4.1). It is quite strange because I compiled the kernel using debian way only 10minutes before and meanwhile I didn't do any updates. Anyway I solved by renaming gcc-4.0 to gcc-4.1 and backing up my "real" gcc-4.1.
[   39.337290] NVRM: loading NVIDIA Linux x86_64 Kernel Module  etc etc etc......

Hope could be useful

---

Every time you change your card you have to reconfigure the Xserver otherwise you will not use the driver for your card.

### Post by crazybus on 2006-12-31
I tried to install the "New Legacy" drivers to work with my Geforce2MX.  However upon reboot it gets to the end of the loading screen then goes to a terminal.  Do you know what my problem is?  Or how to get more information on it.
Thanks in advance for all your help.

---

### Post by tseliot on 2006-12-31
> **crazybus said:**
> I tried to install the "New Legacy" drivers to work with my Geforce2MX.  However upon reboot it gets to the end of the loading screen then goes to a terminal.  Do you know what my problem is?  Or how to get more information on it.
Thanks in advance for all your help.

Ok, let's try to diagnose the error:

Boot with the "nv" driver in your xorg.conf

Then (after Ubuntu has booted) Set the driver to "nvidia" in your /etc/X11/xorg.conf
Log out and Press CTRL+ALT+F1

You will get to the command line (no GUI)
Type:

```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop (if you use GDM)
```
OR
```
sudo /etc/init.d/kdm stop (if you use KDM)
```

Then:
```
startx -- -verbose 5 -logverbose 5
```

You will see several lines with the same error.

Then if you can't get to the command line press CTRL+ALT+F1 or CTRL+C (select NO if it asks you if you want to see the output of the error or something like that)

Then type this in order to copy the output of the error to your home folder:
```
sudo cp /var/log/Xorg.0.log /home/your_username/Xorg.0.log
```
(Of course you have to replace "your_username" with your username)

Access the GNOME or KDE again in the following way:
```
sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
Set the driver back to "nv" instead of "nvidia"
CTRL+O to save
CTRL+X to exit

Then
```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart (if you use GDM)
```
OR
```
sudo /etc/init.d/kdm restart (if you use KDM)
```


Post the content of that file (which you can find under /home/your_username/Xorg.0.log )

### Post by tseliot on 2007-01-01
> **crazybus said:**
> I logged out.  Pressed ALT+CTRL+F1.  This took me to a text mode.  It asked me for my username and password.  I typed these in.  then I typed
sudo /etc/init.d/kdm stop
then, envy
However everything including the errors was the same as before.
Thank you so much for your help.
This is weird... The Xserver doesn't seem to shut down despite our efforts...

Ok, boot in RECOVERY MODE from the GRUB Menu (select it using your keyboard) almost as soon as you turn on your computer (it will take you to the command line).

Then type:
```
envy
```

In the end envy will ask you if you want to restart the Xserver: say no.

then type:
```
reboot
```

and boot as usual

### Post by tseliot on 2007-01-05
> **flapane said:**
> I installed the driver using envy on a gf2mx, even direct rendering is ok, but every time I boot up the pc, it resets the brightness/contrast/gamma setting in nvidia-settings, so every time I have to set them.
How to solve?
Launch nvidia-settings with sudo:
```
sudo nvidia-settings

```

then set what you need and click on the button to save your settings.

    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "i2c"
    Load           "bitmap"
    Load           "ddc"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
    Option         "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
    Option         "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Generic Monitor"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 64.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Generic Video Card"
    Driver         "nvidia"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Generic Video Card"
    Monitor        "Generic Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       4
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       8
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       15
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       16
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
```

I've attached the Xorg.0.log since the post is getting long.  I had to compress it since it was over 19k.  I used gz since I don't know anything about linux archiving.  I've always used 7z.

### Post by tseliot on 2007-01-08
> **FyreBrand said:**
> I've installed the nvidia-glx driver using Alberto's repo.  The install went well and so did the configure with no apparent errors.  I followed the procedure outlined on ubuntu docs.  There are two things I'm not quite understanding.

2. The kde system settings under 'monitor and display' indicates that I'm using a 6800 generic with the 'nv' drivers.

Don't care about what 'monitor and display' says.

### Post by dabl on 2007-01-08

> processor       : 1

model           : 15
model name      : Intel(R) Core(TM)2 CPU         X6800  @ 2.93GHz
stepping        : 6

### Post by lilleulven on 2007-01-11
> Hey, I'm new to Ubuntu and have never installed a specific driver before, I'm interested in installing the latest ATI drivers mentioned on this page (I have a ATI Xpress200M on my laptop) but I don't know how to do it I've been reading this thread and i've seen several ways of doing it, but can someone direct me to the most recent (and correct way) to do it step by step? Thanks.


[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=321766&highlight=ati+radeon+xpress+200M](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=321766&highlight=ati+radeon+xpress+200M)

Before this section:
```

bash ati-driver-installer-8.32.5-x86.x86_64.run --buildpkg Ubuntu/edgy
```

Remember to type in:
```

cd Desktop
```
if that was where you downloaded the .run-file. Just stay in the directory for the rest of the installation/commands.


It seems like user "d-E-a-D" at the top of the page made it work. If you just want the driver, don't move on to the XGL/Beryl section. Cheerio! Hope it works.

### Post by gray-squirrel on 2007-01-15
> **gray-squirrel said:**
> I followed these [instructions]("http://albertomilone.com/instructions.html"), then proceeded to [Method 1 On Line]("http://doc.gwos.org/index.php/Latest_Nvidia_Dapper#METHOD_1_-_ONLINE_INSTALLATION") installation.  I installed a new nVidia GeForce FX 5200 card a few hours ago.

(I was a little concerned because I have integrated graphics on the system board but fortunately, the BIOS detected a card, as did Kubuntu, so I was able to actually to have a Web browser handy {It's VIA's Unichrome}.  I thought I'd be at the command line for a long time but was prepared.)

I did have dependency issues pointed out to me, and I wished I had saved them for posting here.  I went ahead and had aptitude sort it out, which it did without incident.  Then I rebooted, and got 3D acceleration.

An update first, then a question.  I found out later why there were dependency issues: I accidentally pulled the driver from the Edgy repo instead of Dapper.  Everything seemed fine at first, but I had random hard freezes whenever the screen saver kicked in (forcing me to reboot).  I only discovered the source of the problem when I opened [FONT="Courier New"]/etc/apt/sources.list[/FONT] to make a different change.  So, I fixed the file, downloaded the correct driver, rebooted, and everything is golden.

Well, almost.  I can not get Wine to work anymore; I was able to use it before I downloaded the nVidia binary driver.  Even running [FONT="Courier New"]winecfg[/FONT] fails:

Has the use of the binary driver ever been known to break NPTL support?  I've never had this issue when I previously used the Unichrome drivers.  I have not removed the NPTL version of the glibc library (or any others, for that matter).

Other than that everything seems fine. Snippets from the Xorg.log.0 follow:

```

(II) Module glx: vendor="NVIDIA Corporation"
        compiled for 4.0.2, module version = 1.0.9746
        Module class: X.Org Server Extension
        ABI class: X.Org Server Extension, version 0.1

(==) NVIDIA(0): Silken mouse enabled

```

'glxgears -printfps' gives the same error message, and then shows around 1300FPS for a small window (which I can't believe as it's pretty jerky) and around 60FPS for a maximized window (which again I can't believe as it only updates the display once every 2 seconds).

I have the same problem with these latest drivers and with the Edgy standard drivers, and there seems to be no indication of what is actually going wrong here...aside from one obscure error message that I've only ever seen ATI users complain about, and the fact that I don't have DRI and everything is running slowly.

Any ideas what is wrong?

### Post by Adapted.Cat on 2007-01-18
> **Adapted.Cat said:**
> I previously had an ATI card that gave me no end of problems.
So I just got an NVIDIA GeForce FX6200, and now that's giving me problems, too.


Just writing to let you know that I've fixed the problems. I think it was the result of old ATI copies of files still being there from previous installs of fglrx drivers. I did a 'dpkg --force-depends -P' on basically every package that had anything to do with xorg, GL, or DRI before doing 'apt-get -f install' and reinstalling xorg-xserver and nvidia-glx. I also wiped /lib/modules before reinstalling the kernel and restricted modules, but I'm not sure that was necessary. Finally, I ran nvidia-xconfig and startx, and now glxinfo shows direct rendering and glxgears (while it shows the same numbers as it did yesterday) is a whole lot smoother. OpenGL games also work without locking up the machine, which is a vast improvement on my ATI card!

---

After rebooting, all the init scripts failed - that's when I first really became aware of the messages asking for /bin/sh. 

Anyway, I need to get a working system up fast (definitely before the weekend is over and I'll have to do work :P), and I'll look at this unfortunate event as a big lesson to backup not only my data but also my OS from now on, and as a small excuse for upgrading to edgy (I was on dapper).

I hope I'll be able to provide you with useful answers to find out what went wrong. In any case, I will keep the partition that holds the broken install, so I'll be able to do some "forensics".

### Post by Patsoe on 2007-01-22
I don't know where to file the bug (the envy page in Launchpad doesn't accept it), so here goes:

The code in envy_0.8.1-0ubuntu2_all.deb, file classes.py replaces sh->dash by sh->bash, in atipkgmake(), line 241, and at the end of the function changes it back. This sort of makes sense for edgy (which uses dash by default), but it breaks dapper (which doesn't have dash!). Simple fix:

```

def atipkgmake(self):
    if self.details['osname'] == 'edgy':
        os.system('sudo ln -sf bash /bin/sh')
    os.system('bash ' + self.details['drvname'] + ' --buildpkg Ubuntu/' + self.details['osname'])
    if self.details['osname'] == 'edgy':
        os.system('sudo ln -sf dash /bin/sh')

```

where I've added the two if's.

This is still ugly, because it still touches essential files in /bin, which is imho slightly unelegant. If the script gets killed half way, an edgy user ends up with a modified /bin/sh without maybe even knowing it.

Question: why isn't ```
os.system('bash ' + self.details['drvname'] + ' --buildpkg Ubuntu/' + self.details['osname'])
``` sufficient by itself? It explicitly calls bash doesn't it?

### Post by balaji.ramasubramanian on 2007-01-31
Tried nVIDIA driver on V6102AU. Please find the following page for information on the hardware that has been successfully tested. 

[https://wiki.ubuntu.com/LaptopTestingTeam/CompaqPresarioV6102AU](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/LaptopTestingTeam/CompaqPresarioV6102AU)

To test a hardware on Ubuntu, I take quite a bit of time:
1. Installation should be smooth
2. Configuring must be easy and must work - minimal tasks must work fine (Advanced configuration may take time to mature)
3. Intended application must work, related problems thereof must be solved. 

In the case of this driver, all these criteria are met well for the nVIDIA GeForce Go6150 on the HP Compaq Presario V6102AU. Thanks a lot for the efforts involved in writing this driver. How are you funded for this work, Alberto? 

Suggestions for improvement or further questions:

I have a 15inch WXGA display with the nVIDIA card. This has a resolution of 1280x800 right now. I am not sure if even higher resolutions are offered with this TFT screen. How do I know if they are available with the OEM vendor? If other higher ones are available, how do I configure my screen for that?

Best of luck!! 

-Balaji

### Post by jtbo on 2007-02-13
I have Kubuntu dapper 6.06 and Ati Radeon 9550.

I just moment ago downloaded Envy and updated my sources.list.

When I try to install ati driver it says fglrx-kernel-2.6.15-28-386 not found and then Resolving a248.e.akamai.net failed, Connection timed out.

Operation aborted.

Now I do have internet connection alive and kicking. 

Any tips what to do now? -- Don't need tips any more, solved my problem.

Edit:
Here is what I did and succeeded:
Used envy to remove my ati drivers, then still in text mode I installed ati drivers from ati.com, and now I have working 3d acceleration.

### Post by tseliot on 2007-02-16
> **Ubuntiac said:**
> Thanks for the update!

I just went through and updated the repository and followed the ATI instructions again. Everything is still the same as in my last post, including the "mesa" issue.

The only (non wacom) errors in xorg.0.log are:

```
(WW) fglrx: No matching Device section for instance (BusID PCI:1:0:1) found
(WW) fglrx(0): board is an unknown third party board, chipset is supported
(WW) fglrx(0): Option "MergedFB" is not used
(EE) AIGLX error: dlsym for __driCreateNewScreen_20050727 failed (/usr/lib/dri/fglrx_dri.so: undefined symbol: __driCreateNewScreen_20050727)
(EE) AIGLX: reverting to software rendering
```


I'm wondering what this "undefined symbol" line is about...
1) Reboot after the installation f the driver
2) it looks like you'll have to disable AIGLX:
add the following lines to the end of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
```
Section "Extensions"
        Option  "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option  "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection
```
then restart the Xserver and see if it works

Thanks for the great repositories and the work you are doing here.  I am running an AMDturion64 with the ati 200m card, and as of yet no 3d happiness.  I installed the drivers from your newest repositories, but am getting the same error that may others are, still geting the mesa in fglrxinfo:```
Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.5.1)


```

one other factor on my laptop is that i am running linux kernel 2.6.19.2 as I needed to change to get my &#$@ broadcom wifi to work with the 64 bit driver so I wonder if the fglrx driver is being loaded into the wrong module... according to the ati site it looks like the new driver should work ok with the .19 kernel...

this is the same result i've gotten from the other fglrx drivers i've tried to install...

one other thing i notice is that if i run glxgears they run fine for a second, then when my cpu scales up to full speed, they run backwards and lag badly, and the cpu usage goes to near 100%...

I look forward to getting this up and running, I figure if I can get the dang broadcom4318 up on an amd64 hp pavillion, this should be a snap.

thanks ~Ben

### Post by TD-Linux on 2007-02-18
Okay, something really weird is wrong with my Kubuntu :(

I couldn't get ATI 3D acceleration, so I decided to try envy. I found that when I switched to the ctrl-alt-f1 terminal, it was covered with crazy orange lines. I found that all the text terminals through f6 were covered.

I tried to run envy through a konsole, but then it hung on the 'Kubuntu' screen (alt-f1 didn't work).

I then tried using a recovery console, which worked just fine...

But now, my monitor (1600x1200) seems to be getting 1920x1080i! I'll post my totally messed-up xorg.conf if you need it... after I mess it up more...

EDIT: I edited my xorg.conf file to use the old Screen and Device things made by aticonfig. But now it just freezes on the 'Kubuntu' logo again with the statusbar 1/3 of the way (which is where it normally starts X), until I push ctrl-alt-f7. When I push that, the statusbar goes to 0% and freezes :(

### Post by Akegata on 2007-02-18
> **TD-Linux said:**
> I found that when I switched to the ctrl-alt-f1 terminal, it was covered with crazy orange lines. 

In /boot/grub/menu.lst, the kernel line for your default kernel probably includes the word "splash". Remove that and reboot, and you should be rid of that problem.
That fixed it for me anyway.

---

### Post by TD-Linux on 2007-02-18

Okay, I modified the automagic thingy and ran grub-update... not sure if that will help yet...

> **tseliot said:**

Anything else I should do while here?

EDIT: Progress! X boots kinda. The LCD tries 1080i mode first (dunno how that works through DVI) which I can tell because I set that to widescreen, but then switches to normal 1600x1200 mode. The monitor is NOT an IBM G96, which is why that part of the xorg.conf is commented out. However, X quits shortly after...

---

### Post by Akegata on 2007-02-19
'tis a joyous day.

I fooled round with the mess I had previously created, and I finally found the solution.
My problem was that libGL.so.1 was foobar, something I discovered on other places on ubuntuforums, but the replacement versions up for download just didn't work for me, so I eventually searched my drive for another version, and found one in /usr/lib/xorg.

Solution:
```
cd /usr/lib
mv libGL.so.1 $HOME/libGL.so.1.bak
ln /usr/lib/xorg/libGL.so.1 libGL.so.1
```

Restarted the x server, and 3d acceleration is finally back!
Hope this bit of input helps someone else in need.

---

### Post by dannyboy79 on 2007-02-23
This is posted in another thread but I thought I would paste it here as well since more people will probably read this one. I hope that people see my post and are helped by it and maybe I can get some answers from tseliot?

i just used envy for the first time and since I am doing this thru ssh from work, I don't know if it's working yet but I can say that when I did ps aux, it shows gdm is working and dmesg shows 9746. 

Some history on why I went with Envy. Originally I was just using the nvidia driver within dappers repos with my 6200 but in Sept of 2006 I decided to get the latest and greatest. so i first downloaded the 9629 NVIDIA installer from nvidia's website cause I thought that was the way to do it BUT I don't think I used that because for 1 thing my dmesg always said 9631 and when I looked to use the uninstaller when I wanted to go to 9746 I didn't have a folder with an installer file in it. I only had a file called blah blah.run. So I am guessing that I had used albertomilone's 1st repo for 9631 for dapper which was deb [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit) binary/    back in sept of 2006. Well, I updated my kernel and there went my nvidia driver so I needed to install it again. I looked  at [www.albertomilone.com](www.albertomilone.com) and noticed that he changed the repo for the lattest driver so I updated my repo to 
deb [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/latest/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/latest/32bit) binary/     
and did the update and dist-upgrade but x failed stating the API-mismatch and somehting about the nvidia kernel image blah blah. So I investigatyed this and found I needed to uninstall the driver first before updating. Well, I never used the installer, so how can I uninstall the previous driver?? Needless to say I was back and forth between albert's website and the ubuntu forums and thought the easiest way would be to try out envy, use the uninstall function, and then the install function. Well, like I said, since I did all this from work thru ssh, I can't tell for sure if it worked but I think so since ps aux | grep gdm shows gdm is running which I believe is the graphical interface for logging in so I beleive it worked. 

My question now is that during the install using Envy, I saw this error:
unmet dependencies" xserver-xorg-dev: depends on libdrm-dev but will not be installed.
Does this matter?

Also, why did you, tseliot, change your repo for the latest dapper nvidia driver? If i originally used your repo to install the 9631 and then did a kernel update, why did a sudo aptitude update && sudo aptitude dist-upgrade NOT work?? I am only curious? Also, now that I used envy, can I erase your repo from my sources.list? How come you don't put your envy script in your dapper repo? THanks for any answers you can provide. SSL (sorry so long)


edit: IT DIDN'T WORK, I got home and found that I had that blue screen telling me that xserver failed and it was asking me if I want to read the failure report or something or other. I read it and it was saying that the nvidia module wasn't being loaded?? That's weird, I thought the envy script was suppose to install the module? So I simply did a sudo aptitude install nvidia-glx and everything is a-ok now!! Yeah. Still wierd about that Envy script, why wouldn't it have installed the module itself? Maybe tseliot can can answer this.

---

### Post by tseliot on 2007-02-23
> **dannyboy79 said:**
> My question now is that during the install using Envy, I saw this error:
unmet dependencies" xserver-xorg-dev: depends on libdrm-dev but will not be installed.
Does this matter?
Yes, it does.

1) I would like to know how come xserver-xorg-dev depends on another package... Did you use unofficial repositories (other that mine)?

2) can you post the output of this command?
```
sudo aptitude show xserver-xorg-dev
```


> **dannyboy79 said:**
> Also, why did you, tseliot, change your repo for the latest dapper nvidia driver? If i originally used your repo to install the 9631 and then did a kernel update, why did a sudo aptitude update && sudo aptitude dist-upgrade NOT work?? I am only curious?
Have a look at my website and see the new addtess of my repos.  Nvidia added a "new legacy" driver and I had to change the way in which my repos were arranged. That's all.


> **dannyboy79 said:**
> Also, now that I used envy, can I erase your repo from my sources.list? How come you don't put your envy script in your dapper repo? THanks for any answers you can provide. SSL (sorry so long)
If you use Envy you don't need my repositories.


> **dannyboy79 said:**
> edit: IT DIDN'T WORK, I got home and found that I had that blue screen telling me that xserver failed and it was asking me if I want to read the failure report or something or other. I read it and it was saying that the nvidia module wasn't being loaded?? That's weird, I thought the envy script was suppose to install the module? So I simply did a sudo aptitude install nvidia-glx and everything is a-ok now!! Yeah. Still wierd about that Envy script, why wouldn't it have installed the module itself? Maybe tseliot can can answer this.
Without the xserver-xorg-dev Envy can't compile the Nvidia module

#added for automatix 1
deb [http://www.getautomatix.com/apt](http://www.getautomatix.com/apt) dapper main

#AUTOMATIX REPOS START

deb [http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu](http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu) dapper-commercial main

deb [http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) dapper-security main restricted universe multiverse
#AUTOMATIX REPOS END

deb [http://home.eng.iastate.edu/~superm1](http://home.eng.iastate.edu/~superm1) dapper main

Package: xserver-xorg-dev
State: not installed
Version: 1:1.0.2-0ubuntu10.5
Priority: optional
Section: x11
Maintainer: Fabio M. Di Nitto <fabbione@ubuntu.com>
Uncompressed Size: 1683k
Depends: x11proto-core-dev, x11proto-input-dev, x11proto-xext-dev, x11proto-video-dev, x11proto-render-dev,
         x11proto-fonts-dev, libdrm-dev, linux-kernel-headers, x11proto-xf86dri-dev, x11proto-randr-dev
Description: X.Org X server -- development files
 This package provides development files for the X.Org ('Xorg') X server. This is not quite the same as the DDK (Driver
 Development Kit) from the XFree86 4.x and X.Org 6.7, 6.8 and 6.9 series of servers; it provides headers and a pkg-config
 file for drivers using autotools to build against.

 Unless you are developing or building a driver, you probably want xserver-xorg and/or xserver-xorg-core instead.

 More information about X.Org can be found at: <URL:http://xorg.freedesktop.org>
 <URL:http://lists.freedesktop.org/mailman/listinfo/xorg>

 This module can be found as the module 'xserver/xorg' at :pserver:anoncvs@cvs.freedesktop.org:/cvs/xorg

### Post by soxs on 2007-02-23
Hey, I'm very new to Ubuntu and have serious trubble installing the graphics dirvers correctly.

A howto (from the german ubuntu page), which works in most cases.
[Note: Nobody can blame me in any way for damage nor data loss done by using this howto]

Download drivers from ATI page (the .run file->create packages)

Installed these packages/libraries
linux-headers-generic [7]
build-essential
debconf
dh-make
fakeroot
gcc-3.4
libstdc++5
module-assistant (universe, [2]) //* you must add unverse to your apt list

Disable fglrx in /etc/default/linux-restricted-modules-common by adding DISABLED_MODULES="fglrx"

Remove all old drivers you may have installed before for the raphics card

Install xorg-driver-fglrx and fglrx-kernel-source which were the output of the .run file

Now make the kernel use the new driver...
sudo module-assistant prepare 
sudo module-assistant update 
sudo module-assistant a-i fglrx
sudo depmod -a

Make the driver getting used ...
open /etc/X11/xorg.conf
and modify it

//*more lines
Section "Device"
        Identifier      "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 9500 Pro (R300 AD)"
        Driver          "fglrx"
#here may stand ati,change it to fglrx

        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection
//*more lines


additionaly add these lines to disable some stuff which might lead to 3D disableing
(if they allready exist, just add the options to the depending sections)
Section "Extensions"
        Option "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option  "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

save and reboot

After that you should see no mesa when using fglrxinfo.

This is just theorie, in my case it does not work, but it allready helped many.
I have a Radeon 9500 Pro [R300] and in my case it still shows the Mesa realted stuff on fglrxinfo usage.


My current fglrxinfo output:
root@bernhard:/home/bernhard# fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Brian Paul
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa X11
OpenGL version string: 1.5 Mesa 6.5.1

[SIZE="4"]**E[COLOR="DarkRed"]dit: It works!! I just updated xorg and everything is fine!![/COLOR]**[/SIZE]

Now fglrx returns:
root@bernhard:/home/bernhard# fglrxinfo
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: RADEON 9500 Pro Generic
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6286 (8.33.6)

### Post by tseliot on 2007-02-24
> **ZaphodBbl said:**
> Okay.  I've tried Envy  and I've tried the custom drivers you had on your site.  Neither worked on my Radeon 9600XT.  Is there a known problem with the 9600XT?

Looking through Xorg.0.log, I see the following:

drmOpenByBusid: drmGetBusid reports PCI:1:0:0
(EE) AIGLX error: dlsym for __driCreateNewScreen_20050727 failed (/usr/lib/dri/fglrx_dri.so: undefined symbol: __driCreateNewScreen_20050727)
(EE) AIGLX: reverting to software rendering
(II) Loading local sub module "GLcore"
(II) LoadModule: "GLcore"
(II) Loading /usr/lib/xorg/modules/extensions/libGLcore.so
(II) Module GLcore: vendor="X.Org Foundation"
	compiled for 7.1.1, module version = 1.0.0
	ABI class: X.Org Server Extension, version 0.3
(II) GLX: Initialized MESA-PROXY GL provider for screen 0
error opening security policy file /usr/lib/xserver/SecurityPolicy


Could this be the problem, and is there a cure for it?
Add these lines to the end of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
```
Section "ServerFlags"
        Option  "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection
```

then restart the Xserver

Is there a solution out there?  It is RIDICULOUSLY loud.
Be careful - I stopped using XGL/Beryl for this reason since after running XGL/Beryl on an ATI X800XL PCIE over the course of two months the fan on the X800XL completely DIED! I was forunate to find a used X800XL PCIE on eBay for $50 so I picked that up but I'm never going through that again.

When in Windows, the X800 fan throttles down and is not audible for the most part. even in 3d games and apps it throttles gradually and never is at 100%. 

Just be warned that prolonged use of your ATI card with the fan at 100% can potentially shorten the life of it and cause other problems. They need to fix this before I'll use it again.

```

(II) fglrx(0): [pci] find AGP GART
(EE) fglrx(0): [agp] unable to acquire AGP, error "xf86_ENOMEM"
(EE) fglrx(0): cannot init AGP
(II) fglrx(0): [drm] removed 1 reserved context for kernel
(II) fglrx(0): [drm] unmapping 8192 bytes of SAREA 0x2000 at 0xb71a5000

(WW) fglrx(0): * no 3D acceleration available                *
(WW) fglrx(0): ********************************************* *
```

...

Ideas ?
Try adding this line to the Section device of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
```
Option "UseInternalAGPGART" "no"
```

and restart the Xserver

### Post by dejitarob on 2007-03-12
> **tauko said:**
> I've looked for an answer for a long time and I've found none. The problem it's known:

```
~$ fglrxinfo 
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.5.1)
```

[LIST]
[*]/usr/lib/xorg/modules/dri points to /usr/lib/dri
[*]I followed the albertomilone.com guide to install the drivers
[*]I've followed other guides to try to fix the problem, without succes
[*]AIGLX and Composite extension are disabled in xorg.conf
[*]fglrx(0): Acceleration enabled, but GLX: Initialized MESA-PROXY GL provider for screen 0, after loading GLcore module
[/LIST]
I would appreciate any help or ideas. Thanks.

I had the same problem on a ATI Mobility X600 using the 'latest' repos. After spending hours trying different things and reading through posts, I finally got it working by doing the following: 

1. sudo apt-get --purge remove xorg-driver-fglrx linux-restricted-modules-386
2. add the new repos
3. sudo apt-get install linux-restricted-modules-386
4. sudo apt-get install xorg-driver-fglrx (don't know if installing the modules first makes a difference but that's what another guide said to do)
5. sudo ln -s /usr/lib/dri /usr/lib/xorg/modules/dri
6. make sure xorg.conf contains:
```

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option      "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

```
7. edit /etc/modules and add fglrx
8. sudo depmod -a
9. sudo modprobe fglrx
10. reboot
11. run fglrxinfo to test, check /var/log/Xorg.0.log for errors

Thanks tseliot and others for updating the debs and testing!

> Is anyone else having trouble with a recent restricted modules update?

I am running Ubuntu Edgy Eft 64 with an nvidia geforce 6200 card.

The Xorg log file is complaining that the nvidia kernel module cannot be found and when I try to modprobe it manually it gives an error saying it is unable to find the module in /lib/modules/2.6.17-11-generic/volatile

Thanks!

this happened to me also, it is because the module can't be built without xorg-xserver-dev being installed. do ```
sudo aptitude install xorg-xserver-dev
``` and you should be ok after that. (note: i think that it was xorg-xserver-dev, I know for sure it had -dev at the end)

---

### Post by Kevf on 2007-03-12
going crazy here, I've been trying for weeks now and the day before yesterday I've finally got it working with envy. When it worked, I rebooted the system to see if it would still work and it did....

But the next day I was back to the flickering black monitor again. I've decided to do a clean install and give envy another try.

I've enabled the repositories in preferences (edit the binaries) and installed envy. 

The GUI only gives 'error'
The textual one gives 'Your operating system does not seem to be supported by envy'

How can this be? 

I don't want to try other methods because I've got a feeling some methods interfere with each other (because it went back to the old problem). 

But looking at this monitor with a res of  640x480 isn't doable ;)

Someone?

### Post by tseliot on 2007-03-17
> **Kevf said:**
> The GUI only gives 'error'
The textual one gives 'Your operating system does not seem to be supported by envy'

I have temporarily dropped the support for Dapper in series 0.9.x

Remove version 0.9.x and install 0.8.2

---

### Post by Kevf on 2007-03-17
Tnx! Will give it a try this afternoon! :)

edit: It worked! Even after shutting my pc down completely! 
I think one of the other wiki's and tuts I've followed were the cause of the problem. 

(And since I'm here; is there a command for tv-out?)

### Post by tseliot on 2007-03-17
> **Kevf said:**
> Tnx! Will give it a try this afternoon! :)

edit: It worked! Even after shutting my pc down completely! 
I think one of the other wiki's and tuts I've followed were the cause of the problem. 

(And since I'm here; is there a command for tv-out?)

type:
```
sudo nvidia-settings
```

and set the TV out from there

### Post by jellyfisharepretty on 2007-03-17
Just some feedback on the drivers.  Using edgy with an NVIDIA 8800 gts on an Nforce4 chipset.

Method 1 (driver packages found at [http://albertomilone.com/latestrepo.html](http://albertomilone.com/latestrepo.html), used version 9746) do not work.  Vertical green lines appear on the screen before and after the nvidia splash screen.  The computer seems unresponsive after the splash screen, not even able to ctrl-alt-backspace to restart X.  The same drivers were working absolutely fine with my previous card, an nvidia 7800gt.  Even though the 9746 drivers were supposed to support the 8800 series, it didn't work.

Method 2 (official nvidia drivers installer) works.  Used the latest drivers (1.0-9755).  Simply made sure /usr/src/linux pointed to the kernel source of the kernel I'm using, then executed the installer without any command line options. Giving it the x-prefix "--x-prefix=/usr/lib/xorg/" seemed to confuse the installer, as it put the module in the wrong directory.

### Post by tseliot on 2007-03-18
> **zhongwei said:**
> hello

I have tried to use envy and came up with this problem. I copy and paste the whole thing because I am a complete noob and am not sure what is important:
...
Any suggestions? For this problem to be solved do I need to do something else?


A bit of background regarding my system:

AMD64 3700+
nvidia geforce 7600gs sonic (recently replacing a geforce 6600gt, which failed for some unknown reason- luckily still under warranty)

Log out and press CTRL+ALT+F1 (you'll see the command line)

then type:
```
sudo envy
```

NOTE: if Envy seems to hang on the splash screen press ALT+F1

### Post by Kevf on 2007-03-18
Ok this is weird. Just woke up and the pc returned to the same problem: the screen flashes three times  and stays black (working with xp now). 

Yesterday everything just worked fine, even after rebooting the computer and shutting it down. 

I think I know what the problem is (although I don't know what the solution could be). Everynight before I go to bed I switch of the wallsocket (don't know the exact word in english so here is an image)

[img]http://www.pczaak.nl/catalog/images/6-2290.jpg[/img]

Because I hate it when al my devices like modem, dvd-player, cdplayer, stereo etc keep using energy while I'm not using them. But apparently this causes some conflict with linux? I almost can't believe it, but it's the only explenation since I haven't changed a thing........ .:confused:

### Post by dhuff on 2007-03-18
> **doobydave said:**
> @nabdan - Don't think your problem is the same as mine, but hey, nevermind.

Anyone wanna buy a slightly use ATI9600PRO ??

Heh. Yeah...I've come to that conclusion myself - H/W OpenGL acceleration is just *not* gonna happen with my 9600. I've gone back to the 2D 'ati' driver and just given up.

### Post by tseliot on 2007-03-22
> **sbike said:**
> Laptop = brand new thinkpad t60p
OS install:  Edgy, completely stock, with all current patches.

I followed the instructions at:
[http://albertomilone.com/instructions.html](http://albertomilone.com/instructions.html)

Try Envy:
[http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html)

If that doesn't solve the problem add these lines to your /etc/X11/xorg.conf :
 ```
Section "ServerFlags"
        Option  "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection
```

### Post by doobydave on 2007-03-24
@torgrot

Assuming the hot-keys for resolution changing don't work (CRTL ALT + or CTRL ALT - )
and you cannot get into a terminal (CTRL ALT F1) then you will have to revert back to the 'ati' driver.

Boot into the console - recovery mode.

@text login type your user id, then password when prompted.

type

sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

This should open a wizard to rewrite your xorg.conf

The important bit is to select 'ati' on the video driver page.
Most of the other questions you can just hit enter for.

when finished type

sudo /etc/init.d/gdm start

to restart gnome. (may have to hit CTRL ALT F7  to get to gnome display- cant remember)

Good luck by the way....

PS You might want to use the uninstall option from envy to remove fglrx completely if you cannot get it to work.

---

### Post by wncben on 2007-03-27
Finally got my ati issues resolved: I installed Feisty beta, and used the excellent restricted drivers app which automatically loaded the fglrx driver directly without any fuss, just one click of the mouse, simple, and did not require any fancy footwork (though it did revert my display to a lower res, but I simply added it back to /etc/X11/xorg.conf, and voila! )   I spent well over 40 hrs trying to get the various scripts to work for edgy with no sucess, I could not even get fglxgears to sucessfully run, but now I have 1600fps, and tuxracer and planetpenguinracer work smoothly full screen....

Feisty worked for me Y'all, Cheers

### Post by grimmson on 2007-04-06
Hello ts. Thanks for trying so hard to get our systems working. Tried your method with an ATI x1950 pro 512mb version with some success. 2d was fine and I had the same 3d issues as everyone else.  I stumbled across something interesting on ati's website you may already know about.

Note: on the day prior to the 8.32.5 display driver release it was discovered by AMD/Novell that post-X11R72 RC2 does not load the DRM module by default. As a result X.Org 7.2 RC3/final doesn’t look like it will work with the 8.32.5 release, but a workaround may be available.

[http://linux.wordpress.com/2006/12/14/ati-8325-display-drivers-with-xorg-72-support/](http://linux.wordpress.com/2006/12/14/ati-8325-display-drivers-with-xorg-72-support/)

This is a fantastic display of my ignorance, you may already be aware of this. If not it may be an interesting piece of the puzzle. Hope this helps. Keep working on the workaround.

### Post by dannyboy79 on 2007-04-17
For Breezy it was:

sudo dpkg-reconfigure locales

Then you can select whichever locale is native to you.

If you had installed the localeconf package, remove it before running the dpkg-reconfigure:

sudo apt-get remove localeconf

If you're having this problem on dapper/edgy, you may have to regen the locales using

locale-gen <localename>

 where localename is the name of the locale you want to regenerate (e.g. "en_US.UTF-8"), then

dpkg-reconfigure locales

good luck. it also appears by your error that you have 30 upgrades that you're haven't done, any reason??? this may be the reason your missing libraries. i don't think the envy script can do a sudo aptitude update and sudo aptitude upgrade for you. you shoulds always have you sources.list updated and your packages upgraded based on the preferences you have set within synaptic. i am sure this is why you're ahving issues. glad you got it even tually though. so how it mythtv for you in edgy? do you use mythweb? how did you do the upgrade to edgy from dapper, i am about to do that so I can run myth myself. i have 2 xbox frontend and a laptop front end. the backend is a E4300 Core 2 Duo, 1.5 gb ram, GeForece 6200, HP PVR-350, ir blasting a time warner cable box and also receiving input from my atic HD antenna. I love free HD OTA.

### Post by p.robustini on 2007-04-20
I solved the problem of installing Nvidia proprietary drivers by downloading the 9631 version from here: [http://www.nvidia.com/object/linux_display_ia32_1.0-9631.html](http://www.nvidia.com/object/linux_display_ia32_1.0-9631.html)

I first tried the latest drivers, that when Feisty was out was the 9755. X crashed and the error was that the nvidia-kernel-source was 1.0.9361 and the X module was 1.0.9755.
So I looked for the 9631 driver and I installed it.
Now everything works.

Probably the point is there is no nvidia-kernel-source compatible with the latest 9755 drivers...
I wonder...

---

### Post by smsmith050 on 2007-05-09
Replaced K8N Asus mainboard / GeForce 4 MX4000 AGP card running nvidia driver. 
Replacement mainboard was a GA-M61P-S3 GeForce 6100/ NForce 430.
Resolution was 1440x900 on the first boot. 
Monitor V-refresh was 75Hz although preferences screen resolution reported it as 50Hz.
Ran nvidia-xconfig. 
Rebooted and set the resolution to 1440x900 59Hz. Now the monitor reports 60Hz which is correct and the fonts look cleaner. 

The mainboard swap with Ubuntu was so much easier than WIndows. I have not made the sound work yet in XP and,  because microsoft detected  "significant changes" I need to re-authorize XP. I might need to re-install XP and then try to make grub work again. Hate to do that because grub found my W2K, XP, Kubuntu 6.06, and Ubuntu partitions when I installed Ubuntu 7.04.

### Post by dannyboy79 on 2007-05-09
> **smsmith050 said:**
> Replaced K8N Asus mainboard / GeForce 4 MX4000 AGP card running nvidia driver. 
Replacement mainboard was a GA-M61P-S3 GeForce 6100/ NForce 430.
Resolution was 1440x900 on the first boot. 
Monitor V-refresh was 75Hz although preferences screen resolution reported it as 50Hz.
Ran nvidia-xconfig. 
Rebooted and set the resolution to 1440x900 59Hz. Now the monitor reports 60Hz which is correct and the fonts look cleaner. 

The mainboard swap with Ubuntu was so much easier than WIndows. I have not made the sound work yet in XP and,  because microsoft detected  "significant changes" I need to re-authorize XP. I might need to re-install XP and then try to make grub work again. Hate to do that because grub found my W2K, XP, Kubuntu 6.06, and Ubuntu partitions when I installed Ubuntu 7.04.


reinstalling grub is not hard at all and since you know what's going to happen, all you would have to do is save a copy of your menu.lst somewhere other than /boot/grub/ just in case, then install windows and  let it do it's thing and take over the mbr again because winbloz thinks that they're the only os out there! Then just boot to a live cd, open a terminal and do:

```
sudo grub
```
this gets you into the grub command line interface.

```
find /boot/grub/stage2
```

OR IF YOU HAVE A BOOT PARTITION

```
find /grub/stage2
```

this will return something like (hd1,2) (this would be hard drive 2 and partition 3, if that's where your current /boot/grub/ folder's contents are)

then you simply do this

```
root (hd1,2)
```
```
setup (hd1)
```
```
exit
```
then simply make sure that your original menu.lst is still ok (the one with all your boot choices and any other changes you made since you installed ubuntun), if it's not, the easist thing to do would simply move the copy you saved somewhere back into the /boot/grub/ folder using the cp or mv command. then restart the machine without the live cd in and you'll be fine!

Can you believe they want $599 for Windows Vista Ultimate which is what I would need due to all the things like encryption of the hard drive, remote desktop, servers, etc etc. AND UBUNTU IS FREE!!!!!! I love Ubuntu and yes, I will marry it. he he he

### Post by RavanH on 2007-05-11
It has been a while since I started to try for some eye-candy on my desktop (just some shade and maybe transparancy would be nice)... And no positive result so far. Right now I'm just happy that I finally got Suspend working :-| 

I currently have the following setup:
Ubuntu Feisty AMD64
ATI Radeon Xpress 200M (using 128Mb shared mem)
ATI driver version 8.36.5
X.org 7.1 (from Edgy repository)

I reached this point by downgrading X.org 7.2 (Feisty) to 7.1 (Edgy) -- because the ATI propriatery drivers are not   built for 7.2 -- by following the instructions on [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=432554](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=432554)

After that I used Envy to get and build the latest ATI driver... Like I said, still no fancy desktop-effects but at least Suspend works.
